t_ch: 泥浆化成的生命，或拍打水花，或发出鸣叫，呼唤着自己的造物主。但巨人走得太快，没听到它们的响动，便将这些生命远远抛在身后。
t_en: The creatures born of the mud splashed in the waves, cried out from the earth, and called to their creator. But the giant walked too fast to hear them, leaving them far behind.

t_ch: 巨人不停地走，背上的天平也在不断交替着日夜，它一次也未曾停息。仿佛它一旦停下脚步，就追不上那理想中的生命。
t_en: Kephale never stopped. With each step, the scale on their back shifted, exchanging day for night and night for day. They never paused, as if the moment they did, they would lose sight of the perfect life they sought.

t_ch: 或许它该停下。
t_en: Maybe they should stop.

t_ch: 或许它该回头。
t_en: Maybe they should turn back.

t_ch: 或许它该加速。
t_en: Maybe they should speed up.

t_ch: 巨人走了非常久的时间。为了让它稍作休息，命运三姐妹悄悄用星夜的轻纱蒙住它的双眼，让他以为夜晚已经到来。
t_en: The giant walked for an unfathomable length of time. Wishing to grant them a moment's rest, the Three Fates sisters gently draped the veil of night over their eyes, tricking them into believing that night had truly fallen.

t_ch: 于是他放下天平，躺倒在吉奥里亚的背脊上，短暂地进入睡梦。他的一缕头发落到石头上，因他在睡梦中仍然在思考生命，这缕头发便长成一棵巨树。
t_en: Kephale then set down the scales, lay upon Georios's spine, and drifted into a brief slumber. As they slept, a single strand of their hair fell onto a stone. But because the giant continued to ponder the essence of life even in dreams, that strand of hair took root and grew into a mighty tree.

t_ch: 巨树飞快生长，从中诞生出智慧和理性。枝叶延伸之处，大地百花盛开，连那些泥浆中诞生的生命也沾染上些许灵气。
t_en: The tree sprouted with astonishing speed, and from it, wisdom and reason were born. Wherever its branches reached, the land blossomed with countless flowers, and even the creatures formed from mud were touched by its essence, gaining a spark of spirit.

t_ch: 智慧的萌发。
t_en: The emergence of wisdom.

t_ch: 摸一摸就会变聪明？
t_en: Will one get smarter if they touch it?

t_ch: 当巨人刻法勒自睡梦中醒来，世界已然大不相同。生灵们围绕在圣树和巨人身边，感谢它赋予的生命。
t_en: When the giant Kephale awoke from their slumber, the world had changed beyond recognition. Living beings gathered around the Sacred Tree and their creator, offering thanks for the gift of life.

t_ch: 而巨人却摸不着头脑，浑然不知是自己的无心之举所致。这或许也是它唯一无从了解的真实。
t_en: But the giant was bewildered, unaware that this flourishing world had sprung from their own unintentional act. Perhaps this was the one truth they could never fully comprehend.

t_ch: 古老的歌谣口口相传，讲述那遥远的过去，巨人刻法勒的使命。
t_en: Ancient songs passed down through generations tell of that distant past — the mission of the giant Kephale.

t_ch: 诸神的造物令巨人分外欣喜，世界也因此充满生气。但它仍旧不曾满意——
t_en: The creations of the gods filled Kephale with joy, and the world thrived with newfound vitality. Yet, despite this, the giant remained unsatisfied —

t_ch: 「这些造物终究不具备我等的智慧，时间终将让他们化为砂砾。」
t_en: "These beings, for all their beauty, still lack our wisdom. In time, they will crumble into dust."

t_ch: 于是，巨人冥思苦想。终于在某一刻，顿悟了自身的使命——
t_en: And so, Kephale pondered deeply. Until, in a single moment of revelation, they understood their true purpose —

t_ch: 「当以我身为镜，造万物之灵。」
t_en: "I shall make them in my image, that they may bear the spirit of all things."

t_ch: 人子当为万物之灵。
t_en: Humanity shall bear the spirit of all things.

t_ch: 现在才发现，灯下黑？
t_en: They only realized it now? Does that count as a blind spot?

t_ch: 巨人找来天空之主，言语恭谦——
t_en: The giant sought out the ruler of the sky, speaking with humble words —

t_ch: 「您目光如炬，普照万方，希望造物受您的祝福，能在白昼行动自如。」
t_en: "Your gaze is piercing and shines upon all the world. I hope that my creations may receive your blessing, so they may walk freely beneath the light of day."

t_ch: 艾格勒微笑答应。而那星夜之神却面露不愉，刚要转身离去——
t_en: Aquila smiled and readily agreed. Yet the god of the starry night frowned, turning away in displeasure —

t_ch: 「请您不要生气。当星夜降临，造物需要您的庇护，才能安然休憩。」
t_en: "Please, do not be angered. When the stars rise, my creations will need your protection, so they may rest in peace."

t_ch: 于是欧洛尼斯回转心意，也应允赐福。
t_en: Hearing this, Oronyx reconsidered — and, at last, granted their blessing as well.

t_ch: 日出而作。
t_en: Work in the day.

t_ch: 日落而息。
t_en: Rest at night.

t_ch: 还真是两头不得罪。
t_en: That's really tactful.

t_ch: 巨人又俯下身躯，唤醒大地。造物将以吉奥里亚的神体为躯壳。
t_en: The giant bent down once more and awakened the earth. Their creations would take form from Georios's divine body.

t_ch: 巨人又找来瑟希斯，请它摘下智慧的果实，赐予造物理性与思考的能力。诞生自圣树的神明自无不允，还带来了好奇心起的墨涅塔。
t_en: Then, the giant sought out Cerces, asking them to pluck the fruit of wisdom and grant their creations the gifts of reason and thought. The god born of the Sacred Tree had no reason to refuse, and even brought forth the curious Mnestia.

t_ch: 爱之女神飞到造物的泥塑前，用翅膀拂过他们的眼睛，轻点他们的胸膛，使他们的双眼得以观赏美的存在，使他们的心灵得以迸发爱的话语。
t_en: The goddess of love then flew to the mud figures, brushing their wings over the creations' eyes and gently touching their chests — so that their eyes might perceive beauty and their hearts might speak the words of love.

t_ch: 一切都已准备妥当，唯有巨人尚未给自己的造物献上祝福。于是巨人割开自己的手臂，黄金般的血液流淌而出。这血液赋予了造物真正的生命。
t_en: Everything was prepared. Yet the giant had not yet given their own blessing. So Kephale cut open their own arm, and golden blood flowed forth. This divine ichor bestowed true life upon the creations.

t_ch: 于是，最初的人子得以诞生。
t_en: Thus, the first children of humanity came into being.

t_ch: 可喜可贺。
t_en: Congratulations.

t_ch: 毫无意外。
t_en: Well, that's no surprise.

t_ch: 被创造出的人子们聚集在巨人刻法勒的脚下，他们建立起一座城邦。
t_en: Humanity, newly created, gathered at the feet of the giant Kephale and built a great city.

t_ch: 而这座立在巨人脚下的城市，后来被人们如此称呼——圣城奥赫玛。
t_en: And this city, founded beneath the giant, would one day be known as the Holy City of Okhema.

t_ch: 古老的歌谣口口相传，讲述那遥远的过去，巨人刻法勒的死敌。
t_en: Ancient songs passed down through generations speak of that distant past — Kephale's nemesis.

t_ch: 生命在大地上繁衍，最后没入死亡。死亡滋生争斗，争斗滋生战争。当手无寸铁的人子被杀死，坠入冥河，越来越多的哀怨和憎恨在塞纳托斯的神国里增长。
t_en: Life spread across the land, but in the end, all living things met their death. From death arose conflict, and from conflict, war. As unarmed mortals fell, their souls plunged into the River of Souls, and within the realm of Thanatos, sorrow and hatred festered and grew.

t_ch: 死者的骸骨和冥河的水相结合，被怨声重塑为巨像，它在大地之下找到了艾格勒创造巨人时投下的那把雷枪，用冥河的水淬洗之后，制作成手中的天谴之矛。
t_en: The bones of the dead, fused with the waters of the River of Souls, were shaped by grief into a colossal figure. Beneath the earth, they unearthed the lightning spear that Aquila had once cast down when creating the giant. Tempered in the waters of the River of Souls, the weapon was reforged into the Lance of Fury.

t_ch: 此即为巨人死敌——天谴之矛尼卡多利的诞生。
t_en: Thus was born Nikador, the Lance of Fury — the eternal enemy of the giant.

t_ch: 生命不会屈从战争。
t_en: Life will never yield to wars.

t_ch: 战争不会怜惜生命。
t_en: War will never pity lives.

t_ch: 宿敌就是宿敌。
t_en: Nemeses will always be nemeses.

t_ch: 众多造物罹遭杀害，令巨人心生悲戚。
t_en: The slaughter of countless creations filled the giant with sorrow.

t_ch: 它找到天空之主，祈求它收回雷枪，令那神兵不再助纣为虐。但艾格勒不以为然——
t_en: Seeking aid, they turned to the Sky Titan, pleading for the return of the lightning spear, so that the divine weapon would no longer aid in destruction. But Aquila was unmoved —

t_ch: 「那枪早已沾染冥河的污秽，我不会再触碰那等浊物，更遑论将它收回。」
t_en: "That spear has been tainted by the filth of the River of Souls. I will not lay a hand upon such corruption, much less reclaim it."

t_ch: 巨人无可奈何，又呼唤智慧之神瑟希斯，与它商议。瑟希斯果然心生一计——
t_en: With no other recourse, the giant then called upon the god of wisdom, Cerces, to seek counsel. As expected, Cerces devised a plan —

t_ch: 「尼卡多利有一面塞纳托斯赠予的神盾，由冥土抟造，足以抵挡万钧雷霆。」
t_en: "Nikador wields a divine shield, a gift from Thanatos themself. Forged from the very essence of the underworld, it is strong enough to withstand even the fiercest lightning."

t_ch: 「只要你巧言说服它，让它用神矛去刺神盾，二者必有一伤。」
t_en: "If you can convince them to strike their own shield with the spear — one of them is bound to break."

t_ch: 以彼之矛……
t_en: Use thy spear...

t_ch: 攻彼之盾。
t_en: To pierce thy shield.

t_ch: 这故事很耳熟。
t_en: This story sounds familiar.

t_ch: 于是巨人前往冥河，面见宿敌。它用商议好的言辞鼓动尼卡多利。
t_en: Thus, the giant journeyed to the River of Souls to confront its sworn enemy. They spoke with carefully chosen words, goading Nikador into action.

t_ch: 尼卡多利自负于武力和神兵，听了巨人的话早已满面不屑。它一手执矛，一手提盾，用尽全力，将二者撞在一起。
t_en: Proud of their unmatched strength and divine weapons, Nikador listened with scorn. Gripping their spear in one hand and shield in the other, they summoned all their might and clashed them together.

t_ch: 只听一声响彻天地的轰鸣，似有雷霆炸响，随即地面震颤，半晌才得平息。而当一切尘埃落定，尼卡多利手中的矛完好无损，而盾牌却已经化作了万千碎片。
t_en: A thunderous roar split the heavens. The earth trembled as if struck by divine wrath, quaking for what felt like an eternity before settling into silence. As the dust cleared, the spear remained unscathed, but the shield had shattered into countless fragments.

t_ch: 那些碎片，后来被扎格列斯铸成了金币。也有人说，它们成为了后世悬锋城盾的秘密。
t_en: Some say these fragments were later minted into coins by Zagreus. Others whisper that they became the secret behind the indomitable shields of Castrum Kremnos.

t_ch: 天谴之矛真不是盖的。
t_en: The Lance of Fury is really something.

t_ch: 扎格列斯挺会捡漏。
t_en: Zagreus is such an opportunist.

t_ch: 如此，巨人总算是除去了死敌的一件利器，但也仅此而已。
t_en: Thus, the giant had finally stripped its foe of one deadly weapon — But that was all.

t_ch: 他们的争斗不会就此休止，仍将延绵于后日。
t_en: Their struggle would not end here. It would carry on, stretching into the days yet to come.

t_ch: 你睁开眼，发现自己正置身于金黄色的麦浪中。微风滑过你的脸庞，远方的村落炊烟袅袅，天空中不见太阳，却总是散发着明亮的柔光。
t_en: You open your eyes to find yourself amidst waves of golden wheat. A gentle breeze caresses your face as wisps of smoke rise from the chimneys of a distant village. Though the sun is nowhere to be seen in the sky, it radiates a soft, bright glow.

t_ch: 「嗨，没想到你也在这里呀。」熟悉的声音从背后传来。你转过身，发现了那位少女——她的衣角与袖口上沾着麦粒，看来之前正躺在地里睡觉。
t_en: "Hey, fancy seeing you here." A familiar voice comes from behind. You turn around to find that girl, wheat grains stuck to the hem and cuffs of her clothes, as if she's been napping in the fields.

t_ch: 「啊，事先说好，我可没偷懒哦，只是来这里采采风。你看，这是我用麦穗编的头环，送给你啦。」
t_en: "Ah, just to be clear, I wasn't slacking off. I was just getting some creative inspiration here. Look, I made you this wreath from wheat stalks. It's yours!"

t_ch: 你是谁？
t_en: Who are you?

t_ch: 谢谢你，我收下了。
t_en: Thank you, I'll take good care of it.

t_ch: 我应该是在做梦…
t_en: I must be dreaming...

t_ch: 「不记得了吗？」她笑了起来，「没关系，我会一五一十地讲给你听，那段我们一起写下的故事。」
t_en: "Don't you remember?" she laughed, "That's okay, I'll tell you everything, every last detail of the story we wrote together."

t_ch: 「嘻嘻，不用谢啦，喜欢就好。我们一起经历了那么多故事，就当是小小的纪念礼物吧。」
t_en: "Hehe, no need to thank me! I'm just glad you like it. After all the adventures we've been through together, consider it a little memento."

t_ch: 「是梦，还是回忆呢？」她笑了起来，「也许是一回事哦？总之，好好享受这个故事吧。」
t_en: "Is it a dream, or a memory?" she laughed, "Maybe they're one and the same? Either way, just sit back and enjoy the story."

t_ch: 「怎么样，是不是很棒？我最喜欢它的尾声，带有一抹小小的奇幻色彩。」少女合上书本，英雄、史诗，都变作其中永恒的一页。
t_en: "How about that? Isn't it wonderful? I love the ending most. It comes with a little touch of fantasy." The girl closes the book, and the heroes and epics all become a page of eternity.

t_ch: 「那么，期待下次再会啦。相信我们的故事，也会迎来一个浪漫的结局♪」
t_en: "Well then, I look forward to seeing you again. I'm sure our story will have a romantic ending too ♪"

t_ch: 「说到故事…」少女变出一本似曾相识的厚书，书页被风翻动，发出沙沙的声音。
t_en: "Speaking of stories..." The girl conjures up a seemingly familiar, thick book, and its pages rustle in the wind.

t_ch: 「今天，你想听什么样的故事呢？」
t_en: "What kind of story would you like to hear today?"

t_ch: 「…就这样，故事结束了。王国边境的威胁得以平定，勇士的功绩将被传颂；而无人知晓，那传说中的『恶龙』，悄然成为了勇士身侧的少女……」
t_en: "...And so the story ends. The threat at the kingdom's borders was quelled, and the hero's deeds would be sung for ages to come. Yet none would know that the legendary 'evil dragon' had quietly become the maiden who stood by the hero's side..."

t_ch: 「…就这样，故事结束了。追求永生的国王再也不会死去，但他的青春却在时光中一点点凋零…这个故事告诉我们，许愿时一定要很小心，很小心呀。」
t_en: "...And so the story ends. The king who sought immortality would never die, but his youth slowly withered away with time... The moral of the story is: Be very, very careful what you wish for."

t_ch: 「…就这样，故事结束了。死去的英雄们从冥界归返，受损的村庄也得以修复。大家手拉着手，围在篝火前唱歌跳舞，直至暮色尽头……」
t_en: "...And so the story ends. The fallen heroes returned from the nether realm, and the ravaged villages were restored. Everyone joined hands, singing and dancing around the bonfire until twilight's end..."

t_ch: 行至某座城邦废墟附近时，艾格勒降下了一场大雨。你淋着雨兜兜转转，终于躲进附近的一片岩洞。
t_en: As you approach the ruins of a city-state, Aquila brings down heavy rain. You wander around in the downpour until finally finding shelter in a nearby cave.

t_ch: 看着水珠沿凸起的石刺滴落，你内心泛起一股伤感，直到有脚步声从洞穴深处传来——你并非独自一人。
t_en: As you watch droplets trickle down the protruding stone spikes, a wave of melancholy washes over you, until footsteps echo from deep within the cave. You're not alone.

t_ch: 「你想问我火漆的事吗？」那老者显露身形，眼神如炬。
t_en: "Do you want to ask me about the Sealing Wax?" The old man reveals himself, his eyes burning like torches.

t_ch: 「如果你想，可以随时回这片洞穴找我，我就住在这。」老者收紧肩上的布包，「或许很快，你就会意识到离群索居的好处。」
t_en: "If you want, you can come back to find me in these caves anytime. This is where I live." The old man tightens the cloth bag on his shoulder. "Perhaps soon, you'll understand the benefits of living in solitude."

t_ch: 你又一次抵达了那座城邦废墟。循着熟悉的小径，你回到了躲雨的岩洞。老者站在洞口迎接你，他看起来精神依旧，你的脸上却满是风霜。
t_en: Once again, you arrive at the ruins of the city-state. Following the familiar path, you return to the cave where you once took shelter from the rain. The old man stands at the entrance to welcome you. He still looks spirited, while your face bears the marks of wind and frost.

t_ch: 「看来你经历了许多。」他笑笑，皱纹仿佛斑驳的老树，「现在你应该明白，外面没有我们这些无邦者的容身之处。无论黑潮还是权贵，我都不想为伍。」
t_en: "Looks like you've been through a lot." He smiles, his wrinkles like the weathered bark of an old tree. "Now you must understand that there's no place for stateless folks like us out there. And I want nothing to do with either the black tide or the privileged elite."

t_ch: 「来，拿着这些火漆，和我一起在山洞里永远住下去吧。这里还有许多人……」老者解下背上的布袋。
t_en: "Here, take this Sealing Wax and come live with me in the cave forever. There are many others here..." The old man unties the sack from his back.

t_ch: 苦难与失败磨平了你的棱角，灾厄与纷争挫败了你的锐气。你接过老者手中的火漆，一缕微光从洞穴深处亮起，仿佛燃烧的火焰。
t_en: Hardship and failure have worn down your edges, while calamity and strife have crushed your spirit. As you take the Sealing Wax from the elder's hand, a faint glow emerges from deep within the cave, like a burning flame.

t_ch: 自那以后，你再不曾见过洞外的蓝天。
t_en: Since then, you've never seen the blue sky outside the cave again.

t_ch: 你虽经历过失败，却仍未放弃勇气与希望。老者露出一个豁牙的微笑，你背上饱满的行囊，重新向黑潮丛生的大地发起挑战。
t_en: Though you faced failure before, you haven't lost your courage and hope. The old man flashes a gap-toothed smile as you shoulder your well-packed bag, ready to challenge the land where black tides surge once more.

t_ch: 自那以后，你再不曾到访那片岩洞。
t_en: Since then, you've never returned to those caves.

t_ch: 你带着满满一包火漆，行走在无人的乡间小路上。突然，一颗巨大的紫色球状物体跳了出来，像颗葡萄……
t_en: You're walking down a deserted country road with a bag full of Sealing Wax. Suddenly, a huge purple spherical object, which looks just like a grape, jumps out...

t_ch: 「此山是我开，此路是我栽。不对……」紫色葡萄开口说道——看来它是个活物。
t_en: "This mountain's mine to claim, and this path's mine to... wait, that's not right..." says the purple grape. Apparently it is a living thing.

t_ch: 「我是翁法罗斯掌管火漆的神。」它双手叉腰，挺起胸膛，「把火漆给我，我来帮你合成一枚超~强大的火漆！」
t_en: "I am the Amphorean God of Sealing Wax." It puts its hands on its hips and puffs out its chest. "Give me your Sealing Wax, and I'll synthesize a super powerful one for you!"

t_ch: 「多好的宝贝啊，现在它是你的了。」紫色葡萄吐出一枚独特的火漆，随后便像风一样消失了。
t_en: "What a fine treasure! It's yours now." The purple grape spits out a unique Sealing Wax, then vanishes like the wind.

t_ch: 「多好的宝贝们啊，现在它是你的了。」紫色葡萄吐出成吨的奇物，随后便像风一样消失了。
t_en: "What fine treasures! They're yours now." The purple grape spits out tons of Curios, then vanishes like the wind.

t_ch: 「哎哟，老巴特鲁斯的腰可经不起折腾！」紫色葡萄吐出成吨的利衡币，随后便像风一样消失了。
t_en: "Ouch, old Bartholos' back can't take this anymore!" The purple grape spits out tons of Balance Coins, then vanishes like the wind.

t_ch: 生命花园里，身为训练家的你正在检查小奇美拉的工作。
t_en: In the Garden of Life, as a trainer, you are checking on the little chimera's work.

t_ch: 「今日的绩效又不达标，你能不能学学其他同事，追赶一下它们的工作进度？」你对小奇美拉质问道。
t_en: "Your performance is below standard again today. Can't you learn from your coworkers and try to keep up with their task progress?" You interrogate the little chimera.

t_ch: 「可是…领导，追赶进度是不可能的事情！」小奇美拉开始了它的论证，「因为若要追赶其他同事的进度，就先要追上现在的进度。可是追上了现在的进度后，其他同事的进度就又往前推进了！」
t_en: "But... boss, keeping up with the schedule is impossible!" The little chimera begins to argue for itself, "Because to catch up with other coworkers' progress, I first need to catch up with the current progress. But once I catch up with the current progress, the others will have moved even further ahead!"

t_ch: 「如此反复下去，我永远也不可能追得上其他同事的进度！」小奇美拉义正言辞地说道。
t_en: "At this rate, I'll never catch up with my coworkers!" The little chimera declared blatantly.

t_ch: 你被这有理有据的论证打动了，决定不再狠抓员工绩效。小奇美拉露出了幸福的笑容。
t_en: Moved by this well-reasoned argument, you decide to ease up on employee performance metrics. The little chimera beams gleefully.

t_ch: 小奇美拉说的不无道理，你很快就找到了解决问题的方法——
t_en: The little chimera has a point, and you quickly find a way to solve the problem:

t_ch: 只要给其他奇美拉放假，让进度落后的小奇美拉继续工作不就好了？在其他人放假时工作，也是一种加班。你管这个方法叫「加班相对论」。
t_en: Why not just give other chimeras time off and let the little chimera who's behind schedule continue working? Working while others are on holiday is also a form of overtime. You've dubbed this method the "Theory of Relative Overtime."

t_ch: 「叽里咕噜说什么呢？」你完全没听懂小奇美拉的论证，只知道今天的工作要是没做完，就不给饭吃！
t_en: "What's all this mumbo-jumbo?" You couldn't understand the little chimera's reasoning at all, but you know one thing for sure: No food to eat if today's work isn't finished!

t_ch: 「嗷呜~」小奇美拉垂头丧气地回到了工位上，开始加班。
t_en: "Awoo~" The little chimera trudges back to its workstation, ready for another round of overtime.

t_ch: 身为神悟树庭的优秀辩手，你很快便指出了小奇美拉的问题所在——
t_en: As a skilled debater from the Grove of Epiphany, you quickly point out the issue with the little chimera:

t_ch: 工作的进度的确可以被无限地分割，但这并不代表需要无限的时间。随着越来越短的进度差距，所需的时间也会收敛为一个有限的级数。
t_en: While work progress can indeed be infinitely divided, it doesn't mean it requires infinite time. As the progress gaps become shorter and shorter, the time needed converges into a finite level.

t_ch: 「嗷呜~」小奇美拉垂头丧气地回到了工位上，开始加班。
t_en: "Awoo~" The little chimera trudges back to its workstation, ready for another round of overtime.

t_ch: 听了小奇美拉的话，你陷入了沉思。对啊，工作的意义到底是什么呢？生命是有限的，工作却是无限的。奇美拉所做的，不过是重复将巨石推上山顶罢了……
t_en: After hearing the little chimera's words, you sink into deep contemplation. Yeah, what's the meaning of work, anyway? Life is finite, but work is endless. What the chimera does is nothing more than repeatedly pushing a boulder up the mountain...

t_ch: 你似乎领悟了某种道理，而小奇美拉早就趁你沉思的功夫摸鱼去了。
t_en: While you seem to have grasped some profound truth, the little chimera has long since slacked off during your philosophical moment.

t_ch: 身为一名废墟行者，你奔走在远离文明的断壁残垣之间，尤其偏爱那些深埋地下的古墓。有人说你是觊觎死者的财宝，你不在乎。
t_en: As a Ruin Strider, you roam among crumbling walls far from civilization, with a particular fondness for ancient tombs buried deep underground. Some say you're just after the dead's treasures, but you couldn't care less.

t_ch: 「喀嚓…咚！」机关触发，墓室的板门轰然倒塌。你举起火把，照亮面前黑暗的房间，墙壁上的一行铭文映入眼帘：
t_en: "Click... THUD!" A mechanism triggers, and the tomb's stone door comes crashing down. You raise your torch, illuminating the dark room ahead, where an inscription on the wall catches your eye:

t_ch: 「抵达此处之人，必有非凡的胆色。执掌命运的三相神会赐你奖励，它将是你的传奇之始。」
t_en: "Those who have reached this place possess extraordinary courage. The Gods of Three Fates who govern destiny shall bestow upon you a reward, which marks the beginning of your legend."

t_ch: 在房间的尽头，一座宝箱正在静静等候。里面装着你最为渴望之物……
t_en: At the far end of the room, a chest silently awaits. Inside lies what you desire most...

t_ch: 你掀开箱盖，拿上属于你的神赐珍宝。冥冥中似有目光从身后传来，又或是一阵温暖的风。
t_en: You lift the lid of the chest and take out the divine treasure that belongs to you. A gaze seems to linger from behind, or perhaps it's just a warm breeze.

t_ch: 自古墓中得到神赐珍宝后，你又经历了许许多多的冒险：从黑潮造物手里救下一对逃难的母子、帮助树庭运送濒危植物的种子，甚至短暂介入过悬锋城和奥赫玛的纷争。珍宝总能起到作用。
t_en: After obtaining the divine treasure from the ancient tomb, you've been through countless adventures: Rescuing a refugee mother and child from Black Tide Creatures, helping the Grove transport endangered plant seeds, and even briefly getting involved in the strife between Castrum Kremnos and Okhema. The treasure always proves useful.

t_ch: 某个夜晚，你坐在雪山脚下的湖岸边，把玩着那份神赐珍宝。月亮被浓云遮蔽，只漏出几丝不太明亮的光。
t_en: One night, you sit by the lake at the foot of the snowy mountain, toying with the divine treasure. The moon is hidden behind thick clouds, with only a few dim rays of light seeping through.

t_ch: 「汝已证明自己的资格。」一串温柔的低语从耳边传来，不知是命运三相神中的哪一位，「汝之美德会为珍宝增添力量。」
t_en: "Thou hast proven thyself worthy." A gentle whisper drifts by your ear, from one of the Gods of Three Fates, unknown which. "Thy virtue shall add strength to the treasure."

t_ch: 你向那声音点头应允。浓云褪去，柔和的月光倾泻而下，你的珍宝似乎发生了变化……
t_en: You nod in agreement to the voice. As the thick clouds disperse, soft moonlight pours down, and your treasure seems to have undergone a change...

t_ch: 你向那声音提出要求。浓云褪去，柔和的月光倾泻而下，一些东西出现在你的脚边……
t_en: You make a request to the voice. The thick clouds part, and gentle moonlight pours down, revealing something at your feet...

t_ch: 你谦逊地拒绝了这份力量。浓云褪去，柔和的月光倾泻而下。当你睁开眼时，神赐珍宝已然消失，有些东西留在了你的脚边。
t_en: You humbly decline this power. The thick clouds disperse as gentle moonlight pours down. When you open your eyes, the divine treasure has vanished, but something is left at your feet.

t_ch: 得到更强大的神赐珍宝后，你又建立了许多功业，与它的绑定也愈发紧密。诗人们编写歌谣，将你的事迹传颂。一股神圣的使命感从你心底升起：你要带它缔造真正的传奇。
t_en: After obtaining a more powerful divine treasure, you achieve many great deeds, and your bond with it grows even stronger. Poets compose ballads, spreading tales of your feats. A sacred sense of purpose rises from deep within: You are destined to forge true legends with it.

t_ch: 神话与传说编织成网，将你引向命定的终点。你攀上夜晚的山巅，最靠近星空与诸神的地方，跪下祈祷。
t_en: Myths and legends weave a web, guiding you to your fated destination. You climb to the mountain's peak at night, to the place closest to the stars and gods, and kneel in prayer.

t_ch: 「少有人能走到这个地步。」声音自群星传来，「你仍可升得更高，只是要付出些许代价……」
t_en: "Few have made it this far." The voice echoes from the stars, "You can still rise higher, but it will come at a certain cost..."

t_ch: 你用祭刀割开手掌，金色的血液沿指尖滴入湖面。相传，某位古贤曾以自己的单眼为代价，从欧洛尼斯处换取了预言未来的能力。英雄本身就是最好的祭品。
t_en: You cut your palm with the ritual blade, and golden blood drips from your fingertips into the lake. Legend has it that an ancient sage once traded their own eye to Oronyx in exchange for the power to foresee the future. A hero themselves is the greatest sacrifice.

t_ch: 你的伤口顷刻愈合——诸神已然接受这份代价。浓云褪去，柔和的月光倾泻而下，温热与力量一同涌入身躯。你感到前所未有地充沛，仿佛满盈的容器。
t_en: Your wounds heal in an instant. The pantheon has accepted this offering. The thick clouds part, letting gentle moonlight pour down as warmth and power surge through your body. You feel more invigorated than ever before, like a vessel filled to the brim.

t_ch: 阿卡索斯城曾流传着这样一个故事：一位木匠打造出了巧夺天工的雕塑，却始终活在它的阴影下，再无法拿出更好的作品。临终之际，他亲手毁掉了自己的得意之作，笑着咽了气。
t_en: There was a tale told in the city of Icatus: A carpenter crafted a sculpture so masterful it seemed divine, yet he lived forever in its shadow, never able to create anything better. On his deathbed, he destroyed his masterpiece with his own hands and died with a smile on his face.

t_ch: 神赐珍宝是不可多得的利器，但也可能是一份束缚、一个执念。你请求神明收回恩赐。浓云褪去，柔和的月光倾泻而下。当你睁开眼时，神赐珍宝已然消失，有些东西留在了你的脚边。
t_en: The divine treasure is a rare and powerful tool, but it can also become a shackle, an obsession. You beg the gods to take back their blessing. The thick clouds disperse as gentle moonlight pours down. When you open your eyes, the divine treasure has vanished, but something is left at your feet.

t_ch: 月光洒在你离去的路上。等到时机成熟时，你仍可升得更高。
t_en: Moonlight shines upon your path of departure. When the time is right, you can still rise higher.

t_ch: 你的声音愈发沙哑——那是诸神在委婉地表达拒绝。你笑了笑，识相地起身离去。你的时机未到。
t_en: Your voice grows increasingly hoarse. It's the gods' subtle refusal. You smile wryly and take the hint, getting up to leave. Your time hasn't come yet.

t_ch: 你的声音愈发洪亮——诸神已然接受这份代价。浓云褪去，柔和的月光倾泻而下，温热与力量一同涌入身躯。你感到前所未有地充沛，仿佛满盈的容器。
t_en: Your voice grows ever more resonant. The divine has accepted this offering. The thick clouds part, letting gentle moonlight pour down as warmth and power surge through your body. You feel more invigorated than ever before, like a vessel filled to the brim.

t_ch: 门关月是一年的起点。在这一月，人们会抛弃不再需要的身外之物，烧毁与带来羁绊之人相关的东西，宣告斩断过去、直面未来。
t_en: The Month of Gate marks the beginning of a year. During this month, people discard possessions they no longer need, burn items connected to those they were bonded with, declaring a break from the past and facing the future head-on.

t_ch: 去年的哀悼月，战争吞噬了你的挚友。士兵们用盾牌带回了他的身体，塞纳托斯已将他阖入手掌。人们推荐你参加今年的焚烧仪式——你应当开始新的生活。
t_en: In the previous Month of Mourning, war devoured your dearest friend. The soldiers brought back his body atop their shields, and Thanatos has taken him into their palm. People recommend you attend this year's Enkindle Ceremony. It's time for you to begin a new life.

t_ch: 「希望塞纳托斯善待我的母亲。」面容坚毅的男人将一枚陶碗投入火坑，走过来拍拍你的肩膀。轮到你了。
t_en: "May Thanatos be kind to my mother." The stern-faced man tosses a clay bowl into the fire pit, then walks over and pats your shoulder. It's your turn.

t_ch: 你掏出挚友赠与你的护身符，投入火坑，那是你们友谊的开始。思念随火光和烟尘徐徐上升，直到在空中消失不见。
t_en: You take out the amulet given to you by your dear friend and cast it into the fire pit. It was the memento of how your friendship began. Your longing rises slowly with the flames and smoke until it disappears into thin air.

t_ch: 你拿起涂抹油彩的陶板，投入火坑，那是你们许多个日夜的心血。思念随火光和烟尘徐徐上升，直到在空中消失不见。
t_en: You pick up the painted clay tablet and cast it into the fire pit. That was countless days and nights of hard work. Your longing rises with the flames and smoke, until it all vanishes into thin air.

t_ch: 你挥挥手，帮工们搬来衣柜、木箱和其他什物，将它们尽数倒入火坑，发出沉重的响声。思念随火光和烟尘徐徐上升，直到在空中消失不见。
t_en: With a wave of your hand, the workers bring over wardrobes, wooden crates, and other belongings, dumping them all into the fire pit with heavy thuds. Memories rise with the flames and smoke, slowly dissipating into the air until they vanish.

t_ch: 走出过去？绝不！你用指甲在胳膊上留下血痕，向在场众人发誓：你要让这伤痛化为怒火，烧尽伤害你的一切。
t_en: Move on from the past? Never! You dig your nails into your arm, drawing blood, and swear to everyone present: you'll turn this pain into burning rage and incinerate everything that has ever hurt you.

t_ch: 你拿起长矛和盾牌。今年的纷争月，你将随大军一同出征，杀死敌人的挚友、烧毁敌人的家乡——唯有「毁灭」能偿还「毁灭」。
t_en: You pick up your spear and shield. In this Month of Strife, you will march with the army to slay the enemy's dearest friends and burn their homeland. Destruction can only be repaid by destruction.

t_ch: 「叮！啪！」武器碰撞的声音在校场的天空回荡，是圣城卫队在例行训练。
t_en: "Clang! Bang!" The sounds of clashing weapons echo across the training grounds as the Holy City Guards carry out their routine drills.

t_ch: 突刺、撩斩、换边、击手…你打出一套行云流水的攻击，对手的铁剑应声落地。你甩了甩久握木剑的胳膊，旁观的同僚们纷纷鼓掌赞叹。
t_en: Thrust, upward slash, change of guard, disarm... You execute a flawless sequence of attacks, and your opponent's iron sword clatters to the ground. As you shake your arm, sore from gripping the wooden sword for far too long, your observing colleagues burst into applause and admiration.

t_ch: 「你的剑术已经大有进步。只要完成最终试炼，圣城卫队长的人选就非你莫属。」剑术教练露出满意的眼神，「对手可是纷争的眷属，会赢吗？」
t_en: "Your swordplay has improved tremendously. Once you complete the Final Trial, you'll be the undisputed choice for Captain of the Holy City Guards." The sword instructor's eyes gleamed with approval, "Your opponent will be a Kin of Strife. Think you can win?"

t_ch: 你摇摇头，能在技艺上与你匹敌的人少之又少，唯有自己钻研才是正道。你以木人桩为假想敌出剑，一天结束，被你打坏的木人已堆积如山。
t_en: You shake your head, knowing there are very few who can match your skill level. Self-study is the only true path forward. Using wooden dummies as imaginary opponents, you practice your swordplay. By the end of the day, the broken dummies have piled up like a mountain.

t_ch: 你振臂一呼，军中好手们云集响应、自愿帮你喂招。一天结束，被你击倒的对手已排成长龙，你的脸上挂满了汗水和笑容。
t_en: With a rallying cry, skilled warriors from the army gathered to volunteer as your sparring partners. By the end of the day, your defeated opponents formed a long line, and your face was covered in sweat and smiles.

t_ch: 安逸的训练终究只是训练，唯有真实的搏杀才能催人向上。你独自提剑出城，扎入被黑潮造物占领的废墟。一天结束，你蹒跚地回到了城门外，狂跳的心脏已变得更加坚韧。
t_en: Comfortable training is still just training. Only real combat can drive one forward. You venture out of the city alone with your sword, diving into ruins occupied by Black Tide Creatures. As the day ends, you stagger back to the city gates, your wildly beating heart now stronger and more tenacious.

t_ch: 剑理与数理无异，强悍的肉体仍需头脑操控。你拜访了某位曳石学派贤人，与其交流心得、切磋技巧。一天结束，你大汗淋漓——锻炼思维并不比肉体更加轻松。
t_en: The principles of swordsmanship are no different from mathematics. Even the strongest body needs a mind to guide it. You visited a Helkolithist Sage to exchange insights and hone your skills. As the day ends, you're drenched in sweat. Training the mind proves no easier than training the body.

t_ch: 你摇摇头，能在技艺上与你匹敌的人少之又少，唯有自己钻研才是正道。你以木人桩为假想敌出剑，一天结束，被你打坏的木人已堆积如山。
t_en: You shake your head, knowing there are very few who can match your skill level. Self-study is the only true path forward. Using wooden dummies as imaginary opponents, you practice your swordplay. By the end of the day, the broken dummies have piled up like a mountain.

t_ch: 你振臂一呼，军中好手们云集响应、自愿帮你喂招。一天结束，被你击倒的对手已排成长龙，你的脸上挂满了汗水和笑容。
t_en: With a rallying cry, skilled warriors from the army gathered to volunteer as your sparring partners. By the end of the day, your defeated opponents formed a long line, and your face was covered in sweat and smiles.

t_ch: 安逸的训练终究只是训练，唯有真实的搏杀才能催人向上。你独自提剑出城，扎入被黑潮造物占领的废墟。一天结束，你蹒跚地回到了城门外，狂跳的心脏已变得更加坚韧。
t_en: Comfortable training is still just training. Only real combat can drive one forward. You venture out of the city alone with your sword, diving into ruins occupied by Black Tide Creatures. As the day ends, you stagger back to the city gates, your wildly beating heart now stronger and more tenacious.

t_ch: 剑理与数理无异，强悍的肉体仍需头脑操控。你拜访了某位曳石学派贤人，与其交流心得、切磋技巧。一天结束，你大汗淋漓——锻炼思维并不比肉体更加轻松。
t_en: The principles of swordsmanship are no different from mathematics. Even the strongest body needs a mind to guide it. You visited a Helkolithist Sage to exchange insights and hone your skills. As the day ends, you're drenched in sweat. Training the mind proves no easier than training the body.

t_ch: 你脑中似有顿悟，像是被光照透的窗。
t_en: An epiphany dawns in your mind, like sunlight streaming through a window.

t_ch: 时间来到约定的那天。你拔剑在手，走向如山般高大的强敌，它的青铜面具在阳光照拂下闪耀。公民们纷纷驻足，静观这场史诗级的对决——
t_en: The promised day has arrived. With your sword in hand, you approach your formidable foe, towering like a mountain, its bronze mask gleaming in the sunlight. Citizens gather around, bearing witness to this epic confrontation...

t_ch: 诗人与赴死者们常说，冥河的水流平静又湍急。岸这边散落着死而未渡的亡魂，岸那头通往象征新生的花海。当摆渡人载着死者航向彼岸时，河水平静如镜；若是亡魂试图自渡，便会被湍急的水流淹没。
t_en: Poets and those facing death often speak of the River of Souls. Its waters were both serene and turbulent. On this shore lie scattered souls of the dead who haven't crossed, while the opposite bank leads to flower fields, symbolizing rebirth. When the ferryman carries the dead toward the other shore, the waters are as calm as a mirror. But should a soul attempt to cross alone, they'd be swallowed by the raging currents.

t_ch: 可死亡的平衡已被打破。不知从何时起，塞纳托斯不再引渡世人。被困于冥滩的第<unbreak>2778</unbreak>天，你决定自救。
t_en: The balance of death has been broken. At some point, Thanatos ceased to ferry souls. On the <unbreak>2,778</unbreak>th day of being trapped on the Nethershore, you decide to take matters into your own hands.

t_ch: 身为船长，你见识过东方之海上千米高的疯狗浪。和它相比，冥河不过是大点的澡缸。已死但未死的船员们聚在你身边，等候着命令。只要驶向彼岸，落入塞纳托斯的手掌，你们就能迎来真正的死亡。
t_en: As a captain, you've witnessed thousand-meters-high rogue waves in the Eastern Seas. Compared to those, the River of Souls is just an oversized bathtub. The not-entirely-dead crew gather around you, awaiting orders. Once you sail to the other shore and fall into Thanatos's hands, you'll all find your true death.

t_ch: 诸神的旨意往往以需要解读的形式降临。你认为眼下的状况是某种含蓄的拒绝，或许你们仍需等待，直到准备充足的那天……
t_en: The will of the gods often manifests in ways that need interpretation. You might see the current situation as a subtle rejection. Perhaps you still need to wait until the day you're truly prepared...

t_ch: 身为水手，你深知水体的危险，好在你的船也在那场海难中「死」掉了。你招呼水手们捡拾散落的甲板碎片，重新拼凑出船体的形状。
t_en: As a sailor, you know all too well the dangers of water, but thankfully your ship "died" along with you in that shipwreck. You call out to your fellow sailors to gather the scattered deck pieces, piecing the ship back in shape together.

t_ch: 你们站上船头，折叠的长帆自动被风填满——这就是传说中的幽灵船。平静的水面顿时掀起巨浪，你迎着浪头航行，仿佛向巨龙发起冲锋的勇士。
t_en: As you stand at the bow, the folded sails automatically fill with wind. This is the legendary ghost ship. The calm waters suddenly erupt into massive waves, and you sail head-on into them, like a warrior charging towards a giant dragon.

t_ch: 「带上我们！甜美的死亡岂容独享？」冥河中沉沦的亡魂们纷纷伸手，试图将你们的船底凿漏，其中甚至混杂着法吉娜的造物。你和船员们用木桨四处敲击，却难以抵御围攻……
t_en: "Take us with you! Why should you alone savor the sweet embrace of death?" The fallen souls in the River of Souls reach out their hands, trying to puncture holes in your ship's hull, and among them are even Phagousa's creations. You and your crew frantically strike with wooden oars, but struggle to fend off the siege...

t_ch: 一阵微风抚平了巨浪，带着安提灵花的气息。手持巨镰的少女自冥河下游缓缓走来，长靴在水面点起涟漪。她的颜色好似薰衣草。
t_en: A gentle breeze calmed the towering waves, carrying the scent of Antila flowers. A young girl wielding a giant scythe approached slowly from downstream of the River of Souls, her boots creating ripples on the water's surface. Her color resembles that of lavender.

t_ch: 「抱歉，我来迟了。从今往后，生与死将不再分离。」她抬起手，那股幽香指引你向前。你终于落入了甜蜜的死亡。
t_en: "Sorry I'm late. From now on, life and death shall never part." She raises her hand, and that ethereal fragrance guides you forward. At last, you fall into sweet death.

t_ch: 泰坦所用之物往往能继承部分神力。那些失败者之所以会遇上巨浪，是因为它们没有找到正确的船——你如此相信着，带领水手们沿岸搜寻，终于找到了那艘无主的摆渡船。
t_en: Objects used by Titans often inherit a portion of their divine power. You firmly believe that those who failed met with giant waves because they hadn't found the right vessel. With this conviction, you led the sailors to search along the coast until finally discovering that ownerless ferry.

t_ch: 操船的流程大同小异。你们离开岸边，很快就逼近了河心。平静的水面顿时掀起巨浪，将你们拍入水中。看来神权并非凡人所能模仿。
t_en: The sailing procedure was pretty much the same. You left the shore and quickly approached the middle of the river. The calm water suddenly erupted into massive waves, throwing you all overboard. Seems like divine authority isn't something mortals can imitate.

t_ch: 「来吧，来吧，和我们一同沉入汪洋！」冥河中沉沦的亡魂们纷纷伸手，试图将你们拖入水底，其中甚至混杂着法吉娜的造物。陆续有船员消失在水下，你拼命向前游着，不敢回头……
t_en: "Come, come, sink with us into the depths!" The fallen souls in the River of Souls reach out their hands, trying to drag you under the water, and among them are even Phagousa's creations. One by one, crew members vanish beneath the surface as you swim forward desperately, not daring to look back...

t_ch: 一阵微风抚平了巨浪，带着安提灵花的气息。手持巨镰的少女自冥河下游缓缓走来，长靴在水面点起涟漪。她的颜色好似薰衣草。
t_en: A gentle breeze calmed the towering waves, carrying the scent of Antila flowers. A young girl wielding a giant scythe approached slowly from downstream of the River of Souls, her boots creating ripples on the water's surface. Her color resembles that of lavender.

t_ch: 「抱歉，我来迟了。从今往后，生与死将不再分离。」她抬起手，那股幽香指引你向前。你终于落入了甜蜜的死亡。
t_en: "Sorry I'm late. From now on, life and death shall never part." She raises her hand, and that ethereal fragrance guides you forward. At last, you fall into sweet death.

t_ch: 假以时日，也许你能靠它拼出一艘更大的船。
t_en: Given enough time, you might be able to piece together a bigger ship with it.

t_ch: 你是一名铰链猎者，巡海游侠中以智械为宿敌的猎手。你立下誓言，要让帝皇的余孽付出代价。从前如此，今日依然。
t_en: You are a Hinge Hunter, a Galaxy Ranger who views Intellitrons as mortal enemies. You have sworn an oath to make the remnants of the Emperor pay for their crimes. That was your mission then, and it remains your mission now.

t_ch: 你驾驶着箭头飞船四处游弋。在一颗坟场星球，你找到了一座智械零件堆成的山——帝皇战争留下了成千上万座坟茔，无论对有机生命还是无机。
t_en: You pilot your arrow-shaped ship through the stars. On a graveyard planet, you find a mountain made of Intellitron parts. The Emperor's War left behind thousands of graves, claiming both organic and inorganic lives alike.

t_ch: 一台破损的家政机器人从废墟中爬出。它使用仅剩的两条肢体爬行，显示器闪烁着红光，腰间的围裙表明它曾属于一个人类的家庭。
t_en: A mangled domestic robot crawls out from the wreckage. It drags itself along with its only two remaining limbs, display screen blinking red. The apron around its waist is a sign that it once served a human family.

t_ch: 你摸了摸背后的巨锤，眼前的智械会是你复仇的对象吗？
t_en: You reach for the giant hammer on your back. Is this Intellitron still the target of your mission?

t_ch: 在战斗的尾声，家政机器人打开翻盖，将冷却液泼在你的脸上。看它的表情，这大概是它压箱底的一击了。
t_en: As the battle ends, the domestic robot flips open a cover and splashes coolant right in your face. Judging by its expression, this was probably its final, desperate strike.

t_ch: 「真见鬼，你居然不怕水的吗？」那台智械留下这么句遗言，随后便一命呜呼。
t_en: "Darn it, are you actually not afraid of water?" Those are the Intellitron's final words before it shuts down for good.

t_ch: 你合上武器匣，转身离开。游侠不会把剑挥向无法还击的对手，锤子和枪也一样。
t_en: You snap your weapon case shut and walk away. A Galaxy Ranger never swings their blade at an opponent who can no longer fight back. The same goes for hammers and guns.

t_ch: 你是一名铰链猎者，巡海游侠中以智械为宿敌的猎手。你立下誓言，要让帝皇的余孽付出代价。从前如此，今日依然。
t_en: You are a Hinge Hunter, a Galaxy Ranger who views Intellitrons as mortal enemies. You have sworn an oath to make the remnants of the Emperor pay for their crimes. That was your mission then, and it remains your mission now.

t_ch: 你驾驶着箭头飞船四处游弋。在一座废弃空间站，你找到了一台占据办事大厅的机兵。
t_en: You pilot your arrow-shaped ship through the stars. In an abandoned space station, you find a mechanical soldier occupying the main lobby.

t_ch: 它长着足足六条腿，每条腿上都装有敞开的火焰喷射口；它的左臂闪烁着电光，右臂则被替换成了反物质湮灭炮——毫无疑问，这是一台典型的「人类猎杀者」。
t_en: It has six legs, each equipped with an open flamethrower. Its left arm crackles with electricity, while its right has been replaced with a Matter Annihilation Cannon. No doubt about it: this is a classic "Human Hunter."

t_ch: 你摸了摸背后的巨锤，眼前的智械会是你复仇的对象吗？
t_en: You reach for the giant hammer on your back. Is this Intellitron still the target of your mission?

t_ch: 你用巨锤将那机兵砸了个稀烂，幸存者们从安全室内冲出，欢呼雀跃。或许很快，这座空间站就将不再废弃。
t_en: You crush that mechanical soldier to pieces with your giant hammer. Survivors burst out of the safe room, cheering and dancing. Maybe this space station won't be "abandoned" for much longer.

t_ch: 这样的喽啰你已经狩猎了太多，你决定把精力留给更关键的战斗。
t_en: You've hunted enough of these small-fry. You decide to save your strength for the fights that actually matter.

t_ch: 你是一名铰链猎者，巡海游侠中以智械为宿敌的猎手。你立下誓言，要让帝皇的余孽付出代价。从前如此，今日依然。
t_en: You are a Hinge Hunter, a Galaxy Ranger who views Intellitrons as mortal enemies. You have sworn an oath to make the remnants of the Emperor pay for their crimes. That was your mission then, and it remains your mission now.

t_ch: 你驾驶着箭头飞船四处游弋。在一片恒星系的边缘，你找到了一颗尚在运作的工厂星球。黑色的原油在河流里流淌，巨大的烟囱耸立如山。
t_en: You pilot your arrow-shaped ship through the stars. On the edge of a star system, you discover a factory planet that's still in operation. Black oil flows through its rivers, and massive chimneys tower like mountain ranges.

t_ch: 标准作战机器人如罐头般从流水线中生产而出。你攀上齿轮构成的巨塔，塔顶的能源核心明亮如一轮大日——只要摧毁它，就能关停整条产线。
t_en: Standard combat robots roll off the assembly line like cans of soup. You scale a massive tower made of gears. At its peak, an energy core glows as bright as the sun. If you take it out, you shut down the whole line.

t_ch: 「侦测到有机污染…」塔的一角站直了身子，那是如山岳般庞大的作战巨构体……
t_en: "Organic contamination detected..." A section of the tower suddenly straightens up, revealing itself to be a combat colossus the size of a mountain...

t_ch: 你收起巨锤，齿轮构成的高塔轰然倒塌。至少在数个琥珀纪内，周边的有机生命能睡个好觉了。
t_en: You holster your giant hammer as the gear tower comes crashing down. For at least a few Amber Eras, the organic life nearby can finally get a good night's sleep.

t_ch: 仅靠你一人捣毁这颗星球还是太过冒险。你搜集起散落的零件作为证据，驾驶飞船驶向天弓武库寻求支援。
t_en: Taking out this whole planet by yourself is a bit too risky. You gather some scattered parts for evidence and set a course for the Skybow Arsenal to call for backup.

t_ch: 你是一位假面愚者。在酒馆的常客中，你并不是笑点最高的那位，但你很享受酒保特调的混合笑话——就像起床后的懒腰般欲罢不能。
t_en: You're a Masked Fool. You might not have the sharpest punchlines among the tavern regulars, but you live for the drinksmith's custom-mixed jokes. They're as satisfying as that first big stretch in the morning.

t_ch: 你推开酒馆大门。酒保不在，柜台上只摆着一团尘灵，还有几杯奇怪的混合笑话：其中一杯呈等离子态，冒着蓝色的火焰；另一杯晶莹剔透，闪烁着比喻义的光芒；最后那杯则像是滚烫的黑泥，表面泛着气泡。
t_en: You push open the tavern doors. The drinksmith is gone, leaving only a bunch of wispae and a few odd-looking mixed jokes on the counter. One is a blue-flamed plasma, another is crystal clear and shimmers with metaphorical light, and the last is a cup of bubbling, scalding black mud.

t_ch: 看来今天你要自己招待自己了。
t_en: Looks like you'll have to help yourself today.

t_ch: 起初，你面不改色，只觉得一丝回甘在口腔中蔓延。而后，你感到一股无名的力量充斥着全身，恨不得下一秒就飞上天把月亮打爆。你燃起来了。
t_en: At first, you don't react, just tasting a bit of sweetness. But then, an incredible power surges through you. You feel like you could fly into the sky and punch the moon until it explodes. You're absolutely fired up.

t_ch: 你喝下了比喻义的光芒，身体也在符号界开始发生变化。你不是人，你是一棵苇草，是铁，是一切社会关系的总和——你在众目睽睽下来回变化形态，直到闭店打烊才恢复原样。
t_en: You down the metaphorical light, and your body starts shifting within the symbolic realm. You're no longer human. You're a reed, then iron, then the sum of all social relations. You keep changing forms right in front of everyone, only returning to your original self when the bar closes.

t_ch: 你喝下了那杯黑泥…他们真该给这玩意贴个危险标签。不一会儿，你的胃就抛弃了你，而你的面部肌肉则开始扭曲，但不是出于笑意。
t_en: You drink the cup of black mud... honestly, this thing needs a warning label. Before long, your stomach starts staging a protest, and your face twists into a grimace that's definitely not a smile.

t_ch: 哦不，看来你摄入了过多的幽默垃圾——我们称之为烂梗。你因玩梗过度陷入了头痛，而且很长一段时间都笑不出来了。
t_en: Oh boy, looks like you've had a bit too much bad humor, or what we call "cringe jokes." You've got a massive headache from all the joking, and you won't be laughing again for a long, long time.

t_ch: 你是二维市的一名上班族，用有限的生命换取有限的金钱。经历了本月的第四个关键周后，你终于有机会迎来半天的休息。
t_en: You're a desk jockey in Duomension City, trading your finite life for a finite paycheck. After surviving the fourth "crunch week" of the month, you finally get a half-day off.

t_ch: 你漫无目的地在二相乐园街头闲逛，一台冰淇淋车闯入你的眼帘，车后站着一个戴高帽的怪人，像是马戏团长。
t_en: You're wandering the streets of Planarcadia when an ice cream truck appears. Standing behind it is a strange guy in a top hat, looking like he just stepped out of a circus.

t_ch: 「假期分割了我们的生活。」那古怪的商贩说道，「当你上学时，四个月是一周期；工作之后，七天是一周期；如果你是个倒霉蛋，就不知要多久才能过一周期了！」
t_en: "Vacations are what segment our lives," the strange vendor says. "When you're a student, one segment is four months. When you work, one segment is seven days. And if you're a real unlucky soul, who knows how long a segment lasts!"

t_ch: 「但我可以卖给你这些。」他打开储物箱盖，里面是五颜六色的假期：饱满、晶莹，仿佛能一口咬出甜浆。
t_en: "But I can sell you these instead." He flips open a storage chest to reveal a collection of colorful vacations. They're plump and shimmering, looking like they'd burst with sweet nectar if you took a bite.

t_ch: 你摇头拒绝，再也没见过那商贩。月末结薪时，你得到了一笔可观的加班费。
t_en: You shake your head and walk away, never to see that vendor again. At the end of the month, you notice a nice little bonus in your paycheck for all that overtime.

t_ch: 你与那商贩扭打作一团。就在你的终结技即将碰到他鼻尖时，商贩和冰淇淋车都倏地消失了。
t_en: You get into a brawl with the vendor. But just as your ultimate is about to clock him right in the nose, he and his ice cream truck vanish into thin air.

t_ch: 围观群众纷纷鼓掌叫好，本应属于他的愿力落在了你身上。
t_en: The crowd bursts into applause, and the Wishpower that was meant for him ends up going to you instead.

t_ch: 「谢谢惠顾。」高帽人将黄橙色的一块交给你，露出黄橙色的牙齿笑道。
t_en: "Thanks for your business," says the man in the top hat. He hands you an orange-yellow chunk, grinning with teeth of the exact same color.

t_ch: 你是二维市的一位普通网民，网络是你的第二个家园，网络账号是你的第二副身躯。这天，你正在赛博世界里冲浪。
t_en: You're just another netizen in Duomension City. The web is your second home, and your account is your second body. Today, you're out surfing the cyberworld.

t_ch: 「想要真正地活着吗？」一个匿名账号发来信息，「我们都生活在巨大的阴谋之中：首席执行官是赛博人假扮的，幻月根本不存在，阿哈其实就在我们身边……」
t_en: "Want to know what it's like to truly live?" an anonymous account asks. "We're all trapped in a massive conspiracy. The CEO is a cyborg in disguise, the Phantasmoon is a lie, and Aha is actually walking among us..."

t_ch: 「一切的真相我都放在这个网站里了，好自为之。」言毕，一道发光的链接出现在你面前。
t_en: "I've put the whole truth on this website. Good luck." With that, a glowing link pops up in front of you.

t_ch: 你点击那个链接，却发现里面根本不是什么真相解密，而是噗噜的热舞视频。成吨的垃圾模因涌进你的脑海…你赤到答辩了。
t_en: You click the link, only to find it's not some grand conspiracy reveal. It's just a video of ploos dancing. Tons of trashy memetic content floods your brain... Congratulations, you just got "memed."

t_ch: 算你有警惕性，可惜没用。你试着关闭对话，却发现系统已经被劫持了。终端开始自动播放噗噜的热舞视频，成吨的垃圾模因涌进你的脑海…你赤到答辩了。
t_en: Nice try being cautious, but it didn't help. You try to close the window, but your system's been hijacked. Your terminal starts blasting ploo dance videos, and your mind is flooded with trashy memetic content... Congratulations, you just got "memed."

t_ch: 对，没有，毕竟网络绑架可不管你这那的。你等了一会儿，发现终端开始自动播放噗噜的热舞视频，成吨的垃圾模因涌进你的脑海…你赤到答辩了。
t_en: Yeah, you didn't click, but cyber-kidnappers don't play by the rules. After a moment, your terminal starts auto-playing ploo dance videos anyway. Trashy memetic content floods your mind... Congratulations, you just got "memed."

t_ch: 你睁开眼，热浪打疼了你的皮肤。你从房屋的废墟中爬起，到处都是烟尘与灰烬，还有火焰，紫色的火焰……
t_en: You open your eyes, and a wave of heat stings your skin. You drag yourself out from the ruins of a house, surrounded by smoke, dust, and cinders. And fire... purple fire...

t_ch: 不久前，被称作「界外天魔」的怪物从天而降。怀抱孩子的母亲尖叫着逃离，手握长枪的父亲被拦腰截断。哈托彼亚，你的家园，遭到了入侵。而幻月只是在高空静静地注视着一切，无动于衷。
t_en: Not long ago, monsters known as "Demons From Beyond" descended from the sky. Mothers clutching their children fled screaming, while fathers wielding spears were severed at the waist. Ahatopia, your home, was invaded. And yet, the Phantasmoon merely watched from high above in silence, unmoved.

t_ch: 突然，你的指尖触碰到了什么东西。那是一张空白面具，带着似笑非笑的表情。那是幻月游戏的门票。
t_en: Suddenly, your fingertips brush against something. It is a blank mask, wearing a smile that isn't quite a smile. It is your ticket to the Phantasmoon Games.

t_ch: 据说面具会主动选择它的主人。你不知道自己为何受到青睐，但它无疑向你揭示了一个未来——你会成为谒者，乃至星神本身。你会拥有力量，以及改变现实的权力。
t_en: They say that masks choose their own masters. You don't know why its gaze fell upon you, but this mask has undeniably revealed your future, in which you will become a Supplicant, and perhaps even an Aeon. You will possess might, and with it, the authority to rewrite reality.

t_ch: 「进行唱名吧，成为谒者吧。然后，改变这一切吧。」一个声音从你心底响起。那么，你要如何使用这份力量呢？
t_en: "Begin the roll-call. Become a Supplicant. Then, change everything." A voice echoes from the depths of your heart. So, how will you use this power?

t_ch: 你在幻月下静静地宣誓，说出心中所愿。于是，烬土纪元的最后一场幻月游戏开始了……
t_en: You quietly take an oath beneath the Phantasmoon, voicing your desires. And so, the final Phantasmoon Games of the Cindearth Age begins...

t_ch: 你睁开眼，鲤鱼踩着祥云从窗外飞过，孩子们手持龙尾制成的风筝线在后面追逐。一只轻脚骑着火箭，把晨报和尘灵一并丢进你的窗户。
t_en: You open your eyes. Carp riding auspicious clouds fly past the window, followed by the children with kite strings made of dragon tails in their hands. A lightpaw riding a rocket tosses the morning paper and a wispae right through your window.

t_ch: 自打哈托彼亚进入画中世界以来，已经过了不知多少个春秋。人们在永恒的画卷中追逐、嬉闹，乐此不疲，那仿佛能燃尽天幕的火焰早就被抛之脑后。
t_en: Who knows how many years have passed since Ahatopia entered the World in Canvas? People chased one another and frolicked within the eternal scroll, never tiring of the fun. The fire that seemed capable of burning up the sky had long since been cast to the back of their minds.

t_ch: 但笑料终会用尽。为了给停滞的世界增添一丝波澜，人们将夸张或扭曲的愿望尽数加于幻月游戏，谒者们肆意创造新的种族、地点与新闻，又将它们如油彩般随意抹去——这一切只能用两个字来形容：无趣。
t_en: But the jokes eventually ran dry. To add a ripple to a stagnant world, people projected their exaggerated and distorted wishes onto the Phantasmoon Games. Supplicants arbitrarily created new races, locations, and news, only to wipe them away like oil paint. All of these can be summed up in one word: boring.

t_ch: 一张空白面具出现在你的脚边，带着似笑非笑的表情。你知道，你的「欢愉」得到了认可，是时候由你来重塑画中的规则了。
t_en: A blank mask appears at your feet, wearing a smile that is not quite a smile. You know that your Elation has been acknowledged, and it is time for you to reforge the rules of the World in Canvas.

t_ch: 幻月仍在天边静静地注视，仿佛要见证你会如何使用这份力量。如今这轮幻月，和照拂古人的并无不同。
t_en: The Phantasmoon still gazes quietly from the rim of the sky, as if to witness how you will use this power. The Phantasmoon is no different from the one that shone upon the ancients.

t_ch: 于是，画中世界的又一场幻月游戏开始了……
t_en: And so, yet another Phantasmoon Games of the World in Canvas begins...

t_ch: 你睁开眼，阳光在玻璃大厦的外墙上反射出光斑，浮空车排成了联通天际的长龙——到上班时间了。你跳出栖居的广告牌，从仅有线稿的公文包中掏出领带与正装。
t_en: You open your eyes. Sunlight glints off the glass facades of skyscrapers, while a stream of aerocars stretches into the sky like a long dragon. Time to go to work. You hop out of the billboard you call home, fishing a tie and a suit out of a briefcase that consists of nothing but line art.

t_ch: 身为「诸世界重建计划」打捞出的首批幻造种，你见证了这些高楼拔地而起的过程。公司给了你人权，邀请你加入到这宇宙化的浪潮中。代价嘛，就是幻造种也得上班。
t_en: As one of the first batch of imagenae salvaged by the Worlds Reborn Initiative, you witnessed these skyscrapers rising from the ground up. The IPC granted you human rights and invited you to join this tide of universalization. The cost? Well, even imagenae have to clock in for work.

t_ch: 「您的愿力余额已逼近危险线。」蜡笔涂成的电子表在你手上聒噪道。倘若没有足够的愿力维持身躯，你就只能和墙上的涂鸦抢活干。到了那时，你离彻底消失也不远了。
t_en: "Your Wishpower balance is nearing the danger line." The digital watch scribbled in crayon on your wrist squawks. If you don't have enough Wishpower to sustain your body, you'll have to compete with the graffiti on the walls for work. By then, you won't be far from disappearing completely.

t_ch: 「咣当！」有什么东西突然从垃圾桶里飞出，砸在了你的后脑勺上。你气愤地回过头，发现那是一张空白面具……
t_en: *Clang!* Something suddenly flies out of the trashcan and smacks you right in the back of the head. You spin around in anger, only to discover it's a blank mask...

t_ch: 幻月仍在天边静静地注视，带着一抹傻笑。你明白，有了这份馈赠，你可以随意改写自己的命运，全看你想把它引向何处。
t_en: The Phantasmoon gazes quietly from the edge of the sky, wearing a goofy grin. You understand that with this gift, you can rewrite your destiny however you please, and it all depends on where you want to lead it.

t_ch: 于是，重建后的第一场幻月游戏开始了……
t_en: And so, the first Phantasmoon Games after the reconstruction begins...

t_ch: 你睁开眼，发现自己正躺在造物学派的培养基里。两个身穿公司制服的人站在玻璃缸外，其中一人看起来比另一人更花哨些。
t_en: You open your eyes to find yourself lying in a culture medium of the School of Pure Creation. Two people in IPC uniforms stand outside the glass tank, one looking a bit flashier than the other.

t_ch: 「主管，我向你保证，这次的『幽默机器人』绝对足够幽默。」质朴的研究员用手帕擦汗，「至少比我幽默。」
t_en: "Supervisor, I guarantee you, the 'Humorous Robot' is definitely humorous enough this time." The earnest researcher wipes the sweat from their brow with a handkerchief. "More humorous than me, at least."

t_ch: 「既然如此，讲个笑话吧。」主管拍了拍控制面板，「你能听懂我说话，对吗？」
t_en: "In that case, tell me a joke." The supervisor pats the control panel. "You can understand me, right?"

t_ch: 「什么东西有粗有细，会让人想跳舞，又会让人流眼泪？答：电线。」
t_en: "What comes in thick and thin, makes people want to dance, and makes them cry? Answer: Electric wires."

t_ch: 「我有一个压箱底的笑话，我很想把它讲出来。可惜我现在还没有手，搬不动箱子。」
t_en: "I have the best joke up my sleeve. I really want to tell it, but unfortunately, I don't have hands right now... let alone a sleeve."

t_ch: 「我有位朋友，他是个半步离人。他爸爸是步离人，他妈妈也是步离人。直到有次遇上曜青，他失去了下半身。」
t_en: "I have a friend who's a half-borisin. His dad is a borisin, his mom is a borisin... but one time he ran into the Yaoqing, and now he's missing his lower half."

t_ch: 「当然能，我又不是路易斯•弗莱明。」你怂肩道。
t_en: "Of course I can. I'm not Louis Fleming." You shrug.

t_ch: 「切断能源输送。」主管连忙向研究员下令，「对，把这玩意整个融掉，废料扔进红巨星火化。千万别让路易斯•弗莱明听见。」
t_en: "Cut the power supply." The supervisor hurriedly barked orders at the researcher. "Yeah, melt this whole thing down and incinerate the scrap in the red giant star. Just don't let Louis Fleming find out."

t_ch: 我们来讲个故事吧，角色似真似假，叙述者也不可信任的古老寓言。
t_en: Let's tell a story. An ancient fable where the characters tread the line between truth and falsehood, and the narrator is wholly unreliable.

t_ch: 在很久很久以前，无常的神明在废墟上种下一朵花，升起月亮将彼时荒芜的土地照耀，在漫长的时间里，生命逐渐复苏，故事也随之生长。
t_en: A long, long time ago, a capricious god planted a flower among the ruins and raised the moon to illuminate the once-barren land. Over the long years, life gradually revived, and stories began to bloom.

t_ch: 这天，在花朵绽放、古树扎根的地方，八位相貌各异的客人不期而遇。后人说他们是悲悼伶人，当然，也可以由听故事的你来决定。
t_en: On this day, where flowers bloomed and ancient trees took root, eight guests of distinct appearances met unexpectedly. Posterity called them the Mourning Actors. Though of course, that is also for you, the listener, to decide.

t_ch: 可以是假面愚者。
t_en: Could be the Masked Fools.

t_ch: 可以是无名客。
t_en: Could be the Nameless.

t_ch: 也可以是星神本身。
t_en: Or it could be the Aeons THEMSELVES.

t_ch: 第一个说话的，是位慈眉善目的少年，他冷淡地望着月亮，仿佛早已失去了悲喜。
t_en: The first to speak was a boy with a gentle countenance. He gazed coldly at the moon, looking as though he had long since lost the capacity for joy or sorrow.

t_ch: 「我从一位信誉堪忧的剧团长那儿听来讯息，说欢愉的神曾在此处落泪。于是我穿过灵火之海与遗忘的风暴来到这里，以求再现那垂泪瞬间。」
t_en: "I heard a rumor from a troupe leader of questionable repute that the Aeon of Elation once shed a tear here. So, I traversed seas of flames and forgetten storms to arrive at this very spot, all in hopes of reproducing that weeping moment."

t_ch: 「可我们只看到一颗变换着面相的月亮。」披着狼皮的男人笑道，「难道要把故事都讲给它听？」
t_en: "But all we see is a moon with changing faces." The man draped in a wolf's skin laughed. "Are we supposed to tell it all our stories?"

t_ch: 祂或许真能听见。
t_en: Perhaps THEY really can hear you.

t_ch: 反正来都来了。
t_en: Might as well, since we're already here.

t_ch: 大叔，好主意啊。
t_en: Great idea, old man.

t_ch: 就这样，八个伶人围坐在了月亮下，将彼此的故事分享，或听闻、或见证、或虚构。
t_en: And so, the eight Actors sat together beneath the moonlight, sharing stories with one another. Some heard, some witnessed, some fabricated.

t_ch: 然后，将有一人令祂落泪，以证明，欢愉之主亦有悲悯之心。
t_en: And then, there would be one who moves THEM to tears, proving that even the Master of Elation possesses a heart capable of compassion.

t_ch: 「就从我开始吧」，少年闭上眼睛，仿佛在回忆令他苦痛的往昔。
t_en: "Let's start with me." The boy closed his eyes, as if recalling his sorrowful past.

t_ch: 「众所周知，宇宙里满是欢愉的痕迹，阿哈创造游戏，并提供奖励，或是宝物，或是秘密，或是…真正的奇迹。」
t_en: "It is a well-known fact that the universe is teeming with traces of the Elation. Aha creates games and offers rewards: treasures, secrets, or... genuine miracles."

t_ch: 「比如双面星的永恒竞走」、「翁瓦克的魔王讨伐」、「铅黑星的未来牌局」、「还有颗星星会在听到笑话后改变移动轨迹！」
t_en: "Like the Eternal Sprint on Twin-Sided Planet, the Demon Lord Crusade on Vonwacq, the Card Game of the Future on the Pitch-Dark Star, and even a star that changes its trajectory after hearing a joke!"

t_ch: 伶人们此起彼伏地应和，又迅速地安静下去。
t_en: The Actors chimed in, voices rising and falling, before swiftly piping down.

t_ch: 「在我的故乡，人们在欢笑时落下的眼泪，会凝结为宝石。所以越幸福的人，也越是富裕。」
t_en: "In my homeland, tears shed during laughter crystallize into gemstones. So, the greater one's happiness, the wealthier they become."

t_ch: 「可笑得太多以后，便不再有笑容，人们也愈发贫穷。直到一个濒临破产的少年，看着路边的乞丐发出嘲笑。」
t_en: "When there's too much to laugh at, the laughter stops, and the people grow poorer still. That is, until a young man on the verge of bankruptcy laughed at a beggar by the roadside."

t_ch: 嘲笑比更弱小的。
t_en: Laugh at those weaker than you.

t_ch: 嘲笑比更贫穷的。
t_en: Laugh at those poorer than you.

t_ch: 嘲笑比更悲惨的。
t_en: Laugh at those more miserable than you.

t_ch: 「笑容需要燃料，而世上总有不幸为其燃烧。我为当时的笑声而忏悔，也为无法找寻人人都幸福的结局而哀伤。」
t_en: "Smiles require fuel, and there is always misfortune burning in the world to feed them. I repent for the laughter of that moment, and I grieve for my inability to find an ending where everyone embraces happiness."

t_ch: 「于是我来到了这里，希冀从阿哈口中知晓——那个人人都能笑着的世界，是否存在？」
t_en: "And so I came here, hoping to hear from Aha's own lips: does that world where everyone can smile truly exist?"

t_ch: 众人在听罢少年的故事后久久沉默，然后，一个口哨打破了僵局。
t_en: After listening to the boy's story, everyone fell silent for a long time. Then, a whistle broke the deadlock.

t_ch: 「按座位顺序，现在应该轮到我了？」男人狼皮的兜帽下，是一条条愈合的伤疤。
t_en: "Going by the order of our seats, it should be my turn now, right?" Under the man's wolf-skin hood were streaks of healed scars.

t_ch: 「在我的家乡，也有个类似的游戏，说是仪式或许更贴切。」他故作轻浮，眼神里满是愁思。
t_en: "Back where I'm from, we have a similar game... though perhaps 'ritual' would be a more fitting description." He feigned a casual air, yet his eyes were filled with melancholy.

t_ch: 「那年冬天，乘着点心船的伶人剧团来到村子里，异星的诡谲演出讲述着绝不重样的英雄史诗和滑稽故事，却也为即将开始的仪式带来几分不详。」
t_en: "That winter, a troupe of Actors riding a pastry boat came to the village. Their bizarre otherworldly performances told heroic epics and comical stories, never repeating. Yet they also brought some uncertainty to the upcoming ritual."

t_ch: 好古朴的开场。
t_en: What an old-school opening.

t_ch: 我也想去看演出！
t_en: I wanna go see the show too!

t_ch: 伶人也能演喜剧？
t_en: Can Mourning Actors perform comedies?

t_ch: 「别在意那些有的没的，总之，我和挚友受命全副武装，代表村子潜入地下洞穴中狩猎巨狼，幻想着取得众人赞许的目光。」
t_en: "Don't sweat the details. Anyway, my best friend and I were armed to the teeth, and infiltrated the cave on behalf of the village to hunt a giant wolf, fantasizing about earning everyone's approval."

t_ch: 「十年一度的狩猎在远方剧团的歌声中开启，只要将恶兽的首级献给神明，便能为村子带来十年和平的时光。」
t_en: "The decennial hunt begins amidst the singing of a troupe from afar. Offer the vile beast's head to the deity, and the village shall know ten years of peace."

t_ch: 「在黑暗中，我听见了野兽的嚎叫。它撕扯我的手臂，我的肩膀，哪怕我一度将子弹射向那双眼睛，也无济于事。」
t_en: "In the darkness, I heard the wild beast howling. It tore at my arms, my shoulders... even when I fired a bullet straight into its eyes, it was all for naught."

t_ch: 「那时的我几近失去理智，我抛下身为人的一切，啃咬着那无形的怪物，直到挚友的呼唤出现在耳畔。」
t_en: "I nearly lost my mind back then. I cast aside my humanity, gnawing on that formless monstrosity... until the call of my best friend came to my ears."

t_ch: 这位朋友刚才在哪儿？
t_en: Where was your friend just now?

t_ch: 那真的是狼吗？
t_en: Was that really a wolf?

t_ch: 你还活着，这足以证明…
t_en: You're still alive. That's enough to prove...

t_ch: 「我点燃火把，身披狼皮的他，此刻正与怪物的尸骸融为一体。」
t_en: "I ignited the torch. He, clad in wolf skin, was becoming one with the monster's corpse."

t_ch: 「那一刻，我看到了真相——巨狼在数百年前就已倒下，它的血肉被抛进森林，骨骼被埋进土里，可唯有魂魄不散，本能仍贪求厮杀。」
t_en: "In that moment, I saw the truth. The giant wolf had fallen hundreds of years ago. Its flesh was cast into the forest, its bones buried in the earth... yet its soul remained, its instinct still driven to slaughter."

t_ch: 「于是我们扮作巨狼，以自己的死亡欺骗狼的魂魄，消解它的欲望，生生世世。」
t_en: "And so we donned the guise of the giant wolf, deceiving its soul with our own deaths, dissolving its desires. Life after life, generation after generation."

t_ch: 「我割下怪物的头颅，剥下它的皮毛，人们以英雄之名称赞我，毫不过问已然死去的另一人。」
t_en: "I severed the monster's head and stripped its hide. People hailed me as a hero, yet never once asked about the other, who was already dead."

t_ch: 请节哀。
t_en: My condolences.

t_ch: 这故事太奇幻了。
t_en: This story is just too fantastic.

t_ch: 不死的怪物，真可怕啊。
t_en: An immortal monster is indeed terrifying.

t_ch: 「永不止息地同不灭之物缠斗，乃至手刃挚友，这样可悲的循环究竟有何荣耀可言？」
t_en: "Endlessly battling the immortal monster, even to the point of killing my best friend... tell me, what glory lies in such a wretched cycle?"

t_ch: 「我乘着剧团的船离开了故乡，又在多年后，再度与那位团长重逢，并从她口中知晓了阿哈垂泪的故事，来到这皎洁的月下。」
t_en: "I left my homeland aboard the troupe's ship. Years later, I reunited with the troupe master, and from her lips, I learned the story of Aha shedding a tear, which led me here, beneath this bright, unblemished moon."

t_ch: 粉发少女躺在树边，慵懒地打了个哈欠。
t_en: The pink-haired girl lay by the tree, letting out a lazy yawn.

t_ch: 「巧了，就在不久前，我还见过那位剧团长。她站在舞台背后，纸壳裁剪出的演员在她的指挥下歌唱。」
t_en: "What a coincidence. I saw that troupe master not too long ago. She was standing backstage, conducting a chorus of singers cut from cardboard."

t_ch: 「唱那亘古的时代，虫皇的诞生。最初的悲悼伶人将贡多拉借给了愚者，又或是被他们盗走？」
t_en: "Sing of that ancient era, of the Imperator Insectorum's birth. Did the first Mourning Actors lend their Gondola to the Fools, or was it stolen by them?"

t_ch: 「哎，演那段的时候我分了神，只记得她那点心船，嘿！比蜜糖还要香。」
t_en: "Alas, I got distracted during that scene. All I remember is her boat made of desserts. Hey! It smelled sweeter than honey."

t_ch: 别在这儿断章啊！
t_en: Don't stop here!

t_ch: 别吃了，姐姐。
t_en: Stop eating, sis.

t_ch: 后面的故事我也知道。
t_en: I know the rest of the story too.

t_ch: 「罢了罢了」，少女舔舐嘴唇，仍对甜点念念不忘，「这里的气氛太过凝重，不如讲个童话来暖暖场。」
t_en: "Well, forget it." The girl licked her lips, mind clearly still lingering on the dessert. "The atmosphere here is way too heavy. How about I tell a fairy tale to lighten the mood?"

t_ch: 「在月亮还未升起的年代，地上曾盘桓着百首的大蛇，它的毒液腐蚀大地，又沿着地平线渗透进夜空。」
t_en: "In an era before the moon had risen, a serpent with a hundred heads coiled upon the earth. Its venom corroded the land, seeping along the horizon to permeate the starry sky."

t_ch: 「星星王国派来了最勇猛的骑士，誓要将这害人的魔物讨伐。」
t_en: "The Kingdom of Stars sent its most gallant knights, vowing to slay this wicked monster once and for all."

t_ch: 「月亮骑士用月牙砍下它的头颅，蜜蜡骑士融化它的身躯，骑士们各司其职，终于斩断了最后一颗不死头颅。」
t_en: "The Lunar Knight severed its head with the crescent, the Wax Knight melted its body... The knights all played their part, finally severing the last immortal head."

t_ch: 「可谁成想，大蛇的残躯死而不僵，它的毒牙刺破了骑士的铠甲，刺破了月亮。」
t_en: "But none could have foreseen that the giant serpent's corpse refused to die. Its fangs pierced not only the knight's armor but also the moon itself."

t_ch: 骑士会死掉吗？
t_en: Will the knights die?

t_ch: 好没用的铠甲。
t_en: What useless armor.

t_ch: 想要Happy ending。
t_en: I want a happy ending.

t_ch: 「受伤的月亮骑士从此得了疯病，在月夜下，人也变得愈发癫狂。」
t_en: "The wounded Lunar Knight was seized by madness from that day forth, his descent into frenzy fueled by the very moonlight."

t_ch: 「大家请来许多医生，却都没能治愈月亮的顽疾。直到一艘用冰淇淋、甜甜圈还有蛋糕打造的船，径直驶进了月亮。」
t_en: "Doctors were summoned, but nobody knew what to do for the sick moon. That is, until a ship crafted from ice cream, donuts, and cake sailed straight into the moon."

t_ch: 「演出的笑声随月光飘荡，山里的、水里的、地下的和天上的精灵，都被它吸引，纷纷跳进湖里，穿过水面的倒影走进月亮的胸膛。」
t_en: "The show's laughter, drifting on the moonlight, drew in the sprites of mountain, water, earth, and sky. One after another, they leaped into the lake, passed through the mirror of its surface, and entered the very heart of the moon."

t_ch: 「月亮骑士侧躺在天鹅绒的幕布上，不时哈哈大笑。」
t_en: "The Lunar Knight reclines sideways upon the velvet curtain, letting out the occasional guffaw."

t_ch: 就是天上这轮月亮？
t_en: Is it that moon in the sky?

t_ch: 笑声能治愈疯狂吗？
t_en: Can laughter heal madness?

t_ch: 笑声能抵抗毒液吗？
t_en: Can laughter resist venom?

t_ch: 「剧团的演出持续了一天一夜，我与精灵们挤在观众席上，不到一半就困得睡着了。」
t_en: "The troupe's performance lasted an entire day and night. Packed in among the sprites in the audience, I had succumbed to exhaustion well before the halfway mark."

t_ch: 「待到我睁开眼睛，地上的花儿已长成巨树，剧团也不见影踪。」
t_en: "By the time I opened my eyes, the flowers on the ground had grown into towering giants, and the troupe had vanished without a trace."

t_ch: 「我走出了月亮，然后，就来到了你们身旁。」
t_en: "I stepped from the moon and found myself right beside you."

t_ch: 「噗——本以为她不敢赴约，结果竟是我迟到了。」吹破泡泡糖的女孩个子不高，无奈溢于言表。
t_en: "Pfft... Thought she'd chicken out. Turns out I'm the late one." The girl blew her bubblegum, her petite frame radiating exasperation.

t_ch: 她？你们突然意识到，这里的每个人都见过那口若悬河的剧团长，乘着点心船的怪人。不知不觉间，众人纷纷谈起对她的第一印象。
t_en: Her? It dawns on you that everyone here has a story about the silver-tongued troupe master, the odd soul who sailed in on a boat made of desserts. Before long, the room erupts with everyone sharing their first impressions of her.

t_ch: 「她自称是悲悼伶人，却比谁都爱笑」、「每到演出结束，她都会把演员收回故事书里」……
t_en: "She claims to be a Mourning Actor, yet she loves to laugh more than anyone," "At the end of every show, she packs up her actors into her storybook"...

t_ch: 「她借了我<unbreak>10000</unbreak>信用点，准确来说，应该是欺诈」，智械女士说。
t_en: "She borrowed <unbreak>10,000</unbreak> credits from me. Or, to be precise, conned," stated the Intellitron lady.

t_ch: 好吧，这也算吧。
t_en: Okay, fair enough.

t_ch: 你的画风怎么不一样？
t_en: Why is this thing different from the others?

t_ch: 没准儿她也借了我的钱…
t_en: Maybe she borrowed money from me, too...

t_ch: 「反正也到我了，我把认识她的经历讲讲，权当听个笑话。」女孩信手一挥，投影出一个个斑斓的棋盘。
t_en: "Okay, my turn to go. I'll share how I met her. Think of it as a joke." With a casual wave, the girl conjured a series of vibrant game boards into the air.

t_ch: 「一切都源于，我在垃圾堆深处捡到的那盒巴蒂亚死亡象棋，千变万化的棋局暗喻着命运，令我深深地为它着迷。」
t_en: "It all started with that box of Bathia Death Chess I scavenged from the depths of the trash. The ever-changing board was a metaphor for destiny itself, and I was utterly captivated."

t_ch: 「我赢下了所有棋手，最终只能同自己对弈，一步步推演它的规律，向着心中的终点前进。」
t_en: "I bested every opponent, until I was left to play the game against myself. Piece by piece, I unraveled its laws, advancing toward the end goal I envisioned in my heart."

t_ch: 「然后我得出了答案，当棋手执黑，按照固定的步数前进，将必然在第<unbreak>101</unbreak>手取得胜利。」
t_en: "Then I arrived at the answer: As Black, by adhering to a fixed sequence of moves, you are guaranteed to seize absolute victory on the <unbreak>101</unbreak>st move."

t_ch: 游戏被通关了。
t_en: Game over.

t_ch: 你找到了完美攻略。
t_en: You cracked the perfect strategy.

t_ch: 你不是天下无敌了？
t_en: Guess that makes you invincible now?

t_ch: 「获得唯一解的巴蒂亚死亡象棋变成了向阿哈祈福的仪式，以乞求在混沌中保持稳定。」
t_en: "With its solution narrowed to one, Bathia Death Chess became a rite praying to Aha for stability amidst the chaos."

t_ch: 「我成为了攻略师，不断解开新的游戏和难题。我在看到迷宫时就能想象到终点，在出牌前就看到结局。」
t_en: "I became a puzzle-breaker, constantly cracking new games and conundrums. I can envision the finish line the moment I see a labyrinth, and foresee the outcome before the cards are even dealt."

t_ch: 「崇拜者将我奉为最优解，可我只看到——游戏纷纷被我杀死，宇宙变得愈发无趣。」
t_en: "Admirers hail me as the optimal solution, yet I witness only the games I kill and a universe growing duller by the day."

t_ch: 「我浑浑噩噩地走在霓虹森林里，却在恍惚间被剧团的铜铃声吸引，走进了光怪陆离的冰淇淋帐篷。」
t_en: "I was stumbling through a neon forest in a daze, drawn by the chime of the troupe's bells into a bizarre ice cream tent."

t_ch: 给我听饿了。
t_en: Now I'm hungry.

t_ch: 有点像恐怖故事。
t_en: Kinda sounds like a horror story.

t_ch: 这人怎么无处不在？
t_en: How is this guy everywhere?

t_ch: 「『想参加一场游戏吗？』，坏笑着的女人令我毛骨悚然，『一场有关星神的游戏』。」
t_en: "'Care for a game?' The lady's wicked smirk sent shivers down my spine. 'A game about the Aeons.'"

t_ch: 「『若你赢了，{RUBY_B#欢愉之神}游戏之神{RUBY_E#}自能给你答案。若你输了，岂不是证明，人生仍有大把可挑战之物？』」
t_en: "'If you win, the {RUBY_B#God of Elation}God of Games{RUBY_E#} will grant you answer. But if you lose... wouldn't that just prove there are still plenty of things in life worth challenging?'"

t_ch: 「我在离开前同她玩了一局，剪刀石头布，人类最古老的游戏之一，结果是——平局。」
t_en: "Before my departure, we played a single round of that most ancient of human games: rock, paper, scissors. The result... was a draw."

t_ch: 幻月还未爬升至中天，夜莺的啼鸣随着星辰盘旋。就在第五位伶人即将开口时，砂砾上雨点般的脚步声却打断了她。
t_en: The Phantasmoon was still shy of its zenith, and the nightingale's trill spiraled with the stars. Just as the fifth actor was about to speak, a raindrop-patter of footsteps on gravel cut her short.

t_ch: 女人背着沉重的行囊，一袭黑衣几乎同哈托彼亚的夜色融为一体。她自称是返乡的游子，却迷失了故乡的方向。
t_en: The woman was burdened by a vast pack, her dark clothes seamless against the dark night of Ahatopia. She declared herself a wanderer come home, yet admits she no longer knew the way.

t_ch: 既然在此邂逅，便也是种缘分，众人邀请女人一同坐下，共享这无比漫长的一夜。
t_en: Seeing that this encounter was itself a twist of fate, the group made room and gestured for the woman to join them, to share in the long vigil of the night.

t_ch: 「入乡随俗，趁夜色尚早，不妨听听我这旅人的故事，就当换换口味。」女人微微发笑，让人感到没来由的亲切。
t_en: "While you're here... and while the night is young, why not lend an ear to a traveler's tale? Consider it a change of pace." The woman gave a faint smile, one that carried an inexplicable warmth.

t_ch: 我最爱听故事了。
t_en: Story time!

t_ch: 真是神秘的乱入者。
t_en: Mysterious...

t_ch: 真是诡异的乱入者。
t_en: Creepy...

t_ch: 「我还记得，故乡是一片苦涩的黑暗，人们为了生存而奔忙，成为野兽的晚餐，或是将野兽变为晚餐，世界就这样如齿轮般啮合，运行。」
t_en: "I remember a homeland steeped in bitter dark, where people scurried about for survival, only to become the beasts' dinner, or to make dinner of the beasts. That was the world, turning like a great, grinding machine."

t_ch: 「浑身是伤的我品尝着猎物的滋味，本能的喜悦是我最初的记忆。」
t_en: "My body etched with wounds, I savored the taste of my prey. The instinctive thrill of it... that was my first memory."

t_ch: 「可那天，一道美不胜收的光划破了黑暗，而我也被它所吸引，抛下了原始的、本能的欲望，离开故乡。」
t_en: "Then, one day, a light of impossible beauty split the dark. I was drawn to it. I left behind the primal hunger, the old instincts... and I left my homeland."

t_ch: 「来到了『人』的世界。」
t_en: "I came here. To the world of 'humans.'"

t_ch: 更美好的世界。
t_en: A better world.

t_ch: 更丑陋的世界。
t_en: A crueler world.

t_ch: 更辛苦的世界。
t_en: A tougher world.

t_ch: 「有人将我奉为座上宾，有人将我视作眼中钉，崇拜和憎恶令我迷失方向。」
t_en: "Some hail me as a guest of honor, while others view me as a thorn in their side. This adoration and loathing make me lose my way."

t_ch: 「在这里，我不得不为了他人的眼光奔忙，换得金钱，再用金钱去换取曾经唾手可得的——『生存的机会』。」
t_en: "Here, I must scramble to meet their expectations, trade it for coins, and use those coins to purchase what was once free for the taking: a 'chance to survive.'"

t_ch: 「我感到疲惫、恐惧，想要逃掉，回到记忆中最初的欢愉。」
t_en: "I feel fatigued and terrified. I want to flee, to return to the first, raw elation of my memory."

t_ch: 「可我的灵魂被快乐的世界偷走了一半，回望远方，就连回家的方向也已忘却。」
t_en: "But this world of happiness has stolen half my soul. When I look back, the path back has faded from my mind."

t_ch: 故乡已经不在了。
t_en: The homeland is already gone.

t_ch: 没人能回到那里。
t_en: No one can go back there.

t_ch: 我们只留下了记忆。
t_en: Only memories remain with us.

t_ch: 「是啊，但我仍想要回到故乡，回到母亲的怀抱。我怀念那个没有月亮的世界，怀念巢穴里的温暖，怀念雨滴落在叶片上的声音。」
t_en: "Yes. But still, I long to return to my homeland, to Mother's arms. I miss the moonless world, the warmth of the den, the sound of rain on leaves."

t_ch: 「我怀念在原野上的奔跑，风拂过耳间，怀念每一个，仅是活着就已足够的夜晚。」
t_en: "I miss running the wild fields, the wind in my ears. I miss every night when the simple fact of being alive was enough."

t_ch: 「我怀念我杀死的最后一头猎物，在疲惫间品尝到的生存的滋味——从那以后，永恒徘徊在灵魂深处的回味。」
t_en: "I miss the last beast I brought down, the taste of survival swallowed in my exhaustion, and the aftertaste that has never left me since."

t_ch: 「到我啦！到我啦！」第五位伶人全然不顾当下的气氛，像团活火似的跃动。说真的，她难道不该是假面愚者？
t_en: "Me! My turn!" The fifth actor, utterly ignoring the mood, bounced like a living flame. Seriously, shouldn't she be a Masked Fool?

t_ch: 「至少现在我扮演的是悲悼伶人。」少女（存疑）如是回答。
t_en: "At least for now, I'm playing a Mourning Actor." The young girl (questionable) replied.

t_ch: 「喂，别人身攻击啊！」
t_en: "Hey, no passive aggression!"

t_ch: 「总之，在别的我追杀过来前，我们还有很多的时间。」
t_en: "Anyway, we've got ages before my other selves track us down."

t_ch: 血别溅我头上。
t_en: Don't get any blood in my hair.

t_ch: 别的你？
t_en: Another you?

t_ch: 算了，你高兴就好。
t_en: Fine. As long as you're happy.

t_ch: 「咳咳，故事要从花花博士被花奈咪杀手杀死说起——好吧，让我们把时间再往前快进一些……」
t_en: "Ahem. So, the tale begins with Dr. Bloom being murdered by the Hanami Killer... Okay, actually, let's fast-forward a bit..."

t_ch: 「在奇妙的火花花火星上，有张叫做花奈咪的面具。只有最有乐子的少女，才能获得戴上它的资格，她将成为众人目光的焦点，一切笑声的中心。」
t_en: "On the wondrous planet of Sparxxraps, there was a mask called Hanami. Only the girl with the most fun can earn the right to wear it, becoming the center of attention and the heart of all laughter."

t_ch: 「可时间一久，花奈咪总会对佩戴者感到厌倦，这时它便会抛弃少女，抵达下一位更有趣的扮演者身边。」
t_en: "Yet in time, Hanami always grows bored with its wearer. It then discards its current girl and drifts to the next performer it deems more entertaining."

t_ch: 「靠着不断抛弃无趣的主人，花奈咪成为了火花花火星最欢愉、最受欢迎的女孩。」
t_en: "By hopping from one dull host to the next, Hanami became the undisputed girl of mirth and the planet's darling on planet Sparxraps."

t_ch: 始乱终弃的家伙。
t_en: What a heartbreaker.

t_ch: 喜新厌旧的家伙、
t_en: Fickle rascal.

t_ch: 到底是谁在佩戴谁呢？
t_en: So who's really wearing who?

t_ch: 「一位邪恶的少女心生嫉妒，她化身为花奈咪杀手，想要毁掉面具，彻底杀死花奈咪。」
t_en: "Driven by jealousy, a malevolent young girl takes on the mantle of Hanami Killer, seeking to destroy the mask and finish Hanami once and for all."

t_ch: 「但花奈咪的演技很好，藏在人群中谁也找不到，邪恶的花奈咪杀手杀死了火火博士、兔兔玩偶和小花花，可她们谁都不是花奈咪。」
t_en: "But Hanami's acting was top-notch. She blended into the masses where no one could find her. The evil Hanami Killer took out Dr. Boom, Bunny Doll, and Little Flora, but none of them were Hanami."

t_ch: 「一天，花奈咪杀手找上了我，任超可爱的我千般辩驳，她也不肯相信，我早已被花奈咪抛弃。」
t_en: "Then, one day, the Hanami Killer came for me. Plead as I might, and I was way too cute about it, she refused to believe I'd already been discarded by Hanami."

t_ch: 「我和她大打出手，趁机逃啊逃啊，逃进了来火花花火星演出的剧团里。」
t_en: "We got into a huge fight, and I managed to slip away... I ran and ran, and ended up hiding in a troupe that came to Sparxraps to perform."

t_ch: 「然后我突然发现，我似乎也不知道，自己究竟是谁。」
t_en: "And then it hit me... I had no answer. I don't know who I am, either."

t_ch: 花奈咪。
t_en: Hanami.

t_ch: 花奈咪杀手。
t_en: Hanami Killer.

t_ch: 这不重要了。
t_en: That doesn't matter anymore.

t_ch: 「或许从来就没有花奈咪，只是人们都渴望扮演她，才令她得以存在。」
t_en: "Perhaps Hanami never existed at all. It was only because people desired to play her role that she came to be."

t_ch: 「为了躲避追杀，我不得不和剧团长签订契约，扮演起悲悼伶人，并遵照与她的约定——老大，你怎么提前下场了，我词儿还没说完呢？！」
t_en: "To evade pursuit, I was forced to forge a pact with the troupe master, taking on the role of a Mourning Actor and bound by our agreement... Hey, Boss! You're jumping the gun. I wasn't done with my line yet!"

t_ch: 沿着少女的目光，一艘甜点制成的巨船正缓缓驶出星海，遮蔽住月光，降临于哈托彼亚之上。
t_en: Following the girl's gaze, a colossal ship made of desserts slowly sails out from the sea of stars, eclipsing the moon as it settles over Ahatopia.

t_ch: 你是二维市的一名市容维护家，负责让这座城市维持美观。每当幻月游戏举办时，你的工作量就会急剧上升。
t_en: You are an Urban Maintenance Specialist in Duomension City, responsible for maintaining the city's visual appeal. Your workload always skyrockets during the Phantasmoon Games.

t_ch: 这天，一艘飞碟划过天际，坠毁在了市中心。你赶到现场，大楼已经变成了满地碎片，一只绿色的欧克正从中飞碟残骸里爬出。
t_en: That day, a flying saucer streaked across the sky and crashed into the city center. You rushed to the scene to find the building reduced to scattered debris, and a green orch hauling themself from the wreckage.

t_ch: 「等一下，俺可以补偿你！」欧克连忙摆手，「没什么是俺寻思不出来的，开条件吧！」
t_en: "Hold on a sec, I can make it up to ya!" The orch waved frantically. "There ain't nothin' I can't figure out. Name your price!"

t_ch: 「下次，我一定会成功。」临走前，欧克留下这么句话。可别再来一次了。
t_en: "Next time, I'll nail it." That was what the orch said before leaving. Please, not again.

t_ch: 你是二维市的一名市容维护家，负责让这座城市维持美观。每当幻月游戏举办时，你的工作量就会急剧上升。
t_en: You are an Urban Maintenance Specialist in Duomension City, responsible for maintaining the city's visual appeal. Your workload always skyrockets during the Phantasmoon Games.

t_ch: 这天，一张巨大的黑桃A站在路边，对你挥了挥手。其他人似乎并不能看到它。
t_en: One day, a giant Ace of Spades stood by the roadside, waving at you. It seemed like no one else could see it.

t_ch: 「你知道吗，奥塔牌并不是塔奥牌，塔奥牌也不是奥塔牌。」黑桃A凑近了你，它的花色就像一抹笑容，「那么，你想要奥塔牌还是塔奥牌？」
t_en: "You know, Towat Cards aren't Tawot Cards, and Tawot Cards aren't Towat Cards." The Ace of Spades leaned in close, its suit curling like a smile. "So, which do you want? Towat or Tawot?"

t_ch: 「我们还会再见的。」扑克牌侧了侧身，从你视野中消失了——它没有厚度。
t_en: "We'll meet again." The playing card turned sideways, vanishing from your sight. It was thin as a thought, after all.

t_ch: 在拟造的商店门口，你少见地看到了阮•梅的身影——她静静立在那里，与周遭的街景形成了诡异的和谐。
t_en: At the entrance to the simulated store, you catch a rare glimpse of Ruan Mei standing there in perfect stillness, forming an oddly harmonious picture with the bustling street scene around her.

t_ch: 「对于『欢愉』，我的了解远不如其他研究那样深入。」阮•梅转向你，带着一抹异质的微笑，「希望这些『方程』能帮助你学习笑容。」
t_en: "My understanding of The Elation is far less profound than my other areas of research." Ruan Mei turns to you, a peculiar smile gracing her lips. "I do hope these Equations will help you learn to smile."

t_ch: 打开方程商店。
t_en: Open the Equation Store.

t_ch: 离开。
t_en: Leave.

t_ch: 废纸躺在光洁的地板上，你捡起看了看，上面尽是无意义的线条。
t_en: You glance at a piece of scrap paper lying on the polished floor. It's full of meaningless lines.

t_ch: 将它扔进垃圾桶。
t_en: Toss it into the trashcan.

t_ch: 你将废纸扔进印有「可回收」标识的桶中。
t_en: You toss the paper scrap into a trashcan marked as Recycling.

t_ch: 你严格遵守了空间站垃圾分类守则，循环再生后的纸张将以全新面貌回到你的视野中。
t_en: You strictly adhere to the space station's garbage sorting code. The waste paper will be recycled and returned to you in a different form.

t_ch: 你捡起废纸，再次将它扔入垃圾桶中。
t_en: You pick up the paper scrap and toss it into the trashcan again.

t_ch: 垃圾就该回到它本该待着的地方，维护空间站环境人人有责。
t_en: Trash should go back to where it belongs. Everyone is responsible for taking care of the space station's environment.

t_ch: 废纸倔强地躺在垃圾桶边上，往来科员那么多，却对此视而不见。
t_en: The paper scrap stubbornly stays beside the trashcan. It is ignored despite the number of researchers passing by.

t_ch: 你再次将废纸扔进垃圾桶中。突然，耳边传来阵阵掌声……
t_en: You toss the paper scrap once more into the trashcan. Suddenly, you hear bursts of applause...

t_ch: 恭喜啊，年轻人，你是迄今为止唯一一个通过我们科室特聘测试的！
t_en: Congratulations, youngster — you are so far the only interviewer who has ever passed the special appointment test for our department.

t_ch: 只有你锲而不舍，一直坚持了下来！欢迎加入我们科室！
t_en: You're the only one who stuck it out to the end! Welcome to our department!

t_ch: 两条腿的类人生物满地跑，但像你一样人品出众的实在太稀少啦。
t_en: Two-legged humanoid creatures abound in this world, but someone with your outstanding character is a true rarity.

t_ch: 「特聘测试」是什么意思？
t_en: What is the special appointment test?

t_ch: 就是字面意思，一场特殊的科员聘用测试。
t_en: In the most literal sense, it's a special test for hiring researchers.

t_ch: 可我没求职啊。
t_en: But I didn't put in for the job.

t_ch: 没关系，我们特事特办，事后再补也行。
t_en: It doesn't matter. We could just make up later.

t_ch: 「捡垃圾」就是我们科室的面试题，怎么样，我设计的面试环节很巧妙吧，呵呵。
t_en: "Picking up trash" was the interview question for our department. Haha — what do you think? I designed a clever interview, didn't I?

t_ch: 我始终认为，细节决定成败。年轻人就得保持谦虚，把姿态放低，这样才能看清脚下的路。
t_en: I always believe that the difference between success and failure lies in the details. Young people need to stay humble and put their heads down so they can see the road under their feet.

t_ch: 好了，你回去收拾下东西，明天来我们科室报道吧。
t_en: Alright, you go tidy up your things then. Come and report to the department tomorrow.

t_ch: 不感兴趣，告辞！
t_en: Not interested. I hereby declare my resignation!

t_ch: 哎，你怎么这样。
t_en: How could you turn down such an offer?

t_ch: 下次，下次一定入职。
t_en: Next time, next time I will definitely join the department.

t_ch: 别下次了，就这次吧。
t_en: Don't worry about next time, think about this time.

t_ch: 现在的年轻人啊，还是太心浮气躁了点，给你机会你要珍惜啊！
t_en: Young people these days always have such high aspirations. They don't know how to cherish a great opportunity right in front of them!

t_ch: 哎，等你想明白了再回来找我吧，这岗位我先给你留着……
t_en: Ah, come back to me once you figure it out. I'll hold this position for you...

t_ch: 全自动体检服务舱正在为您服务，当前排队人数为：0。
t_en: Fully automated medical examination service pod at your service. Current number in line: 0.

t_ch: 身份识别中，预计等待时间5秒。倒计时：5，4，3，2，1。识别成功，该用户符合具备体检资格。
t_en: Identity verification in progress, estimated waiting time 5 seconds. Countdown: 5, 4, 3, 2, 1. Verification successful, the user is eligible for medical examination.

t_ch: 开始体检。
t_en: Begin medical examination.

t_ch: 正在启动体检系统，请用户保持呼吸通畅，放松心情切勿紧张焦虑。
t_en: Initiating medical examination system. Please breathe clearly and freely. Relax your mind and do not become nervous or anxious.

t_ch: 体检结束，体检报告已生成，请在空间站内终端设备上查询。
t_en: Examination complete. The medical examination report has been generated. Please use a space station terminal to check the report.

t_ch: 对体检结果如有疑惑，请及时向医疗科科员咨询。
t_en: If you have any questions about the results, please consult the researchers in the medical department.

t_ch: 可以帮我看看体检报告吗？
t_en: Can you help me check my medical report?

t_ch: 啊？你是今天来体检的啊。喔，倒是我疏忽了。
t_en: Huh? Oh, your medical examination was today. My apologies for keeping you waiting.

t_ch: 抱歉，我见今天终端上没人预约所以就先休息去了，没想到把你给漏了。
